utf.tarl

T.A.R.L. — Thirsty's Active Resistance Language

 1"""T.A.R.L. — Thirsty's Active Resistance Language"""
 2from utf.tarl.analyzer import (
 3    AnalysisResult,
 4    ConflictPair,
 5    CoverageGap,
 6    PolicyAnalyzer,
 7    ShadowedRule,
 8)

10from utf.tarl.composer import CompositionError, PolicyComposer
11from utf.tarl.explainer import PolicyExplanation, RuleTrace, TarlExplainer
12from utf.tarl.runtime import TarlRuntime
13from utf.tarl.spec import (
14    DEFAULT_DENY,
15    CompositionOp,
16    SetOp,
17    TarlDecision,
18    TarlPolicy,
19    TarlPolicyRef,
20    TarlPolicySet,
21    TarlProof,

24)

class TarlVerdict(enum.StrEnum): 
18class TarlVerdict(StrEnum):
19    DENY = "DENY"
20    ESCALATE = "ESCALATE"
21    ALLOW = "ALLOW"
22
23    def __str__(self) -> str:
24        return self.value
25
26    def __lt__(self, other: object) -> bool:
27        if not isinstance(other, TarlVerdict):
28            return NotImplemented
29        return _VERDICT_RANK[self] < _VERDICT_RANK[other]
30
31    def __le__(self, other: object) -> bool:
32        if not isinstance(other, TarlVerdict):
33            return NotImplemented
34        return _VERDICT_RANK[self] <= _VERDICT_RANK[other]
35
36    def __gt__(self, other: object) -> bool:
37        if not isinstance(other, TarlVerdict):
38            return NotImplemented
39        return _VERDICT_RANK[self] > _VERDICT_RANK[other]
40
41    def __ge__(self, other: object) -> bool:
42        if not isinstance(other, TarlVerdict):
43            return NotImplemented
44        return _VERDICT_RANK[self] >= _VERDICT_RANK[other]
45
46    @staticmethod
47    def meet(a: TarlVerdict, b: TarlVerdict) -> TarlVerdict:
48        """Restrictive composition (∧): min in safety ordering."""
49        return a if _VERDICT_RANK[a] <= _VERDICT_RANK[b] else b
50
51    @staticmethod
52    def join(a: TarlVerdict, b: TarlVerdict) -> TarlVerdict:  # type: ignore[override]
53        """Permissive composition (∨): max in safety ordering.
54
55        Intentionally shadows ``str.join`` (this enum subclasses ``str``); it is
56        the lattice join over verdicts, not string concatenation.
57        """
58        return a if _VERDICT_RANK[a] >= _VERDICT_RANK[b] else b
DENY = <TarlVerdict.DENY: 'DENY'>
ESCALATE = <TarlVerdict.ESCALATE: 'ESCALATE'>
ALLOW = <TarlVerdict.ALLOW: 'ALLOW'>
@staticmethod
def meet( a: TarlVerdict, b: TarlVerdict) -> TarlVerdict: 
46    @staticmethod
47    def meet(a: TarlVerdict, b: TarlVerdict) -> TarlVerdict:
48        """Restrictive composition (∧): min in safety ordering."""
49        return a if _VERDICT_RANK[a] <= _VERDICT_RANK[b] else b

Restrictive composition (∧): min in safety ordering.

@staticmethod
def join( a: TarlVerdict, b: TarlVerdict) -> TarlVerdict: 
51    @staticmethod
52    def join(a: TarlVerdict, b: TarlVerdict) -> TarlVerdict:  # type: ignore[override]
53        """Permissive composition (∨): max in safety ordering.
54
55        Intentionally shadows ``str.join`` (this enum subclasses ``str``); it is
56        the lattice join over verdicts, not string concatenation.
57        """
58        return a if _VERDICT_RANK[a] >= _VERDICT_RANK[b] else b

Permissive composition (∨): max in safety ordering.

Intentionally shadows str.join (this enum subclasses str); it is the lattice join over verdicts, not string concatenation.

@dataclass
class TarlDecision: 
174@dataclass
175class TarlDecision:
176    """Result of evaluating a T.A.R.L. policy against a context."""
177    verdict: TarlVerdict
178    reason: str = ""
179    rule_index: int = -1
180    matched_rule: str | None = None
181    expires_at: str | None = None    # ISO-8601 UTC; set for time-bound verdicts
182
183    def __str__(self) -> str:
184        s = f"[{self.verdict.value}] {self.reason}"
185        if self.expires_at:
186            s += f" (expires: {self.expires_at})"
187        return s
188
189    def is_expired(self) -> bool:
190        """
191        Return True if this decision carried a time-bound verdict that has
192        now elapsed.  Always False when expires_at is not set.
193
194        The engine stamps expires_at at evaluation time but never
195        re-evaluates automatically.  Callers that cache decisions must call
196        this method before acting on a stored result and re-evaluate when it
197        returns True.
198        """
199        if not self.expires_at:
200            return False
201        import datetime
202        try:
203            exp = datetime.datetime.fromisoformat(
204                self.expires_at.replace("Z", "+00:00")
205            )
206            if exp.tzinfo is None:
207                exp = exp.replace(tzinfo=datetime.UTC)
208            return datetime.datetime.now(datetime.UTC) > exp
209        except ValueError:
210            return False

Result of evaluating a T.A.R.L. policy against a context.

TarlDecision( verdict: TarlVerdict, reason: str = '', rule_index: int = -1, matched_rule: str | None = None, expires_at: str | None = None)
verdict: TarlVerdict
reason: str = ''
rule_index: int = -1
matched_rule: str | None = None
expires_at: str | None = None
def is_expired(self) -> bool: 
189    def is_expired(self) -> bool:
190        """
191        Return True if this decision carried a time-bound verdict that has
192        now elapsed.  Always False when expires_at is not set.
193
194        The engine stamps expires_at at evaluation time but never
195        re-evaluates automatically.  Callers that cache decisions must call
196        this method before acting on a stored result and re-evaluate when it
197        returns True.
198        """
199        if not self.expires_at:
200            return False
201        import datetime
202        try:
203            exp = datetime.datetime.fromisoformat(
204                self.expires_at.replace("Z", "+00:00")
205            )
206            if exp.tzinfo is None:
207                exp = exp.replace(tzinfo=datetime.UTC)
208            return datetime.datetime.now(datetime.UTC) > exp
209        except ValueError:
210            return False

Return True if this decision carried a time-bound verdict that has now elapsed. Always False when expires_at is not set.

The engine stamps expires_at at evaluation time but never re-evaluates automatically. Callers that cache decisions must call this method before acting on a stored result and re-evaluate when it returns True.

@dataclass
class TarlPolicy: 
117@dataclass
118class TarlPolicy:
119    """Ordered decision function: P = [r₁, r₂, ..., rₙ] over context space."""
120    rules: list[TarlRule] = field(default_factory=list)
121    source: str = ""
122    name: str = "unnamed"
123    # Phase 2: composition
124    parent: str | None = None
125    composition: CompositionOp | None = None
126    includes: list[TarlPolicyRef] = field(default_factory=list)
127    has_stop: bool = False
128    # Phase 5: temporal governance
129    version: str | None = None
130    supersedes: str | None = None
131    valid_from: str | None = None      # ISO-8601; policy not effective before this
132    valid_until: str | None = None     # ISO-8601; policy expires after this
133    on_expiry: TarlVerdict | None = None  # verdict when outside window (default ESCALATE)
134    if_unresolved_after: int | None = None  # seconds; auto-expire from valid_from
135    revert_to: str | None = None            # policy name to evaluate on succession
136
137    def __str__(self) -> str:
138        header = f"policy {self.name}"
139        if self.composition and self.parent:
140            header += f" {self.composition.value} {self.parent}"
141        if self.version:
142            header += f" v{self.version}"
143        lines = [f"{header}:"]
144        if self.valid_from:
145            lines.append(f"  valid_from: {self.valid_from}")
146        if self.valid_until:
147            lines.append(f"  valid_until: {self.valid_until}")
148        if self.supersedes:
149            lines.append(f"  supersedes: {self.supersedes}")
150        if self.on_expiry:
151            lines.append(f"  on_expiry: {self.on_expiry.value}")
152        if self.if_unresolved_after is not None and self.revert_to:
153            ds = self.if_unresolved_after
154            if ds % 3600 == 0:
155                dur = f"{ds // 3600}h"
156            elif ds % 60 == 0:
157                dur = f"{ds // 60}m"
158            else:
159                dur = f"{ds}s"
160            lines.append(f"  if_unresolved_after: {dur} => revert_to: {self.revert_to}")
161        for ref in self.includes:
162            alias_part = f" AS {ref.alias}" if ref.alias else ""
163            if ref.is_file:
164                lines.append(f'  INCLUDE "{ref.name}"{alias_part}')
165            else:
166                lines.append(f"  INCLUDE {ref.name}{alias_part}")
167        for r in self.rules:
168            lines.append(f"  {r}")
169        if self.has_stop:
170            lines.append("  STOP")
171        return "\n".join(lines)

Ordered decision function: P = [r₁, r₂, ..., rₙ] over context space.

TarlPolicy( rules: list[TarlRule] = <factory>, source: str = '', name: str = 'unnamed', parent: str | None = None, composition: CompositionOp | None = None, includes: list[TarlPolicyRef] = <factory>, has_stop: bool = False, version: str | None = None, supersedes: str | None = None, valid_from: str | None = None, valid_until: str | None = None, on_expiry: TarlVerdict | None = None, if_unresolved_after: int | None = None, revert_to: str | None = None)
rules: list[TarlRule]
source: str = ''
name: str = 'unnamed'
parent: str | None = None
composition: CompositionOp | None = None
includes: list[TarlPolicyRef]
has_stop: bool = False
version: str | None = None
supersedes: str | None = None
valid_from: str | None = None
valid_until: str | None = None
on_expiry: TarlVerdict | None = None
if_unresolved_after: int | None = None
revert_to: str | None = None
@dataclass
class TarlRule: 
 96@dataclass
 97class TarlRule:
 98    """A single `when <condition> => VERDICT [for: <duration>]` rule."""
 99    condition: str
100    verdict: TarlVerdict
101    source_line: int = 0
102    duration_seconds: int | None = None  # time-bound verdict ("for: 4h")
103
104    def __str__(self) -> str:
105        s = f"when {self.condition} => {self.verdict.value}"
106        if self.duration_seconds:
107            ds = self.duration_seconds
108            if ds % 3600 == 0:
109                s += f" for: {ds // 3600}h"
110            elif ds % 60 == 0:
111                s += f" for: {ds // 60}m"
112            else:
113                s += f" for: {ds}s"
114        return s

A single when <condition> => VERDICT [for: <duration>] rule.

TarlRule( condition: str, verdict: TarlVerdict, source_line: int = 0, duration_seconds: int | None = None)
condition: str
verdict: TarlVerdict
source_line: int = 0
duration_seconds: int | None = None
@dataclass
class TarlPolicyRef: 
88@dataclass
89class TarlPolicyRef:
90    """A reference to another policy in a composition directive."""
91    name: str
92    alias: str | None = None
93    is_file: bool = False

A reference to another policy in a composition directive.

TarlPolicyRef(name: str, alias: str | None = None, is_file: bool = False)
name: str
alias: str | None = None
is_file: bool = False
@dataclass
class TarlPolicySet: 
213@dataclass
214class TarlPolicySet:
215    """
216    A named composition of multiple policies evaluated with set operators.
217
218    groups: list of (SetOp, [policy_name, ...])
219    Each group produces a verdict via its operator.
220    The final verdict is the meet (∧) of all group verdicts.
221    """
222    name: str
223    groups: list[tuple[SetOp, list[str]]] = field(default_factory=list)
224    default_verdict: TarlVerdict = TarlVerdict.DENY
225    source: str = ""
226
227    def __str__(self) -> str:
228        lines = [f"policy_set {self.name}:"]
229        for op, names in self.groups:
230            lines.append(f"  combine {op.value} [{', '.join(names)}]")
231        lines.append(f"  default: {self.default_verdict.value}")
232        return "\n".join(lines)

A named composition of multiple policies evaluated with set operators.

groups: list of (SetOp, [policy_name, ...]) Each group produces a verdict via its operator. The final verdict is the meet (∧) of all group verdicts.

TarlPolicySet( name: str, groups: list[tuple[SetOp, list[str]]] = <factory>, default_verdict: TarlVerdict = <TarlVerdict.DENY: 'DENY'>, source: str = '')
name: str
groups: list[tuple[SetOp, list[str]]]
default_verdict: TarlVerdict = <TarlVerdict.DENY: 'DENY'>
source: str = ''
@dataclass
class TarlProof: 
235@dataclass
236class TarlProof:
237    """
238    Integrity certificate of a policy evaluation.
239
240    Π = (H(P), H(c), k, v, T, σ)
241      H(P) — SHA-256 of exact policy source used
242      H(c) — SHA-256 of canonical context snapshot
243      k    — matched rule index (-1 = DEFAULT_DENY)
244      v    — final verdict
245      T    — evaluation trace: [{rule_index, condition, matched}, ...]
246      σ    — optional HMAC-SHA256 tag or Ed25519 signature over the canonical
247             encoding
248
249    Proofs are **unsigned by default**. HMAC-SHA256 remains available as a
250    symmetric compatibility mode: it is forgeable by any party that holds the
251    key, including a verifier. Use Ed25519 for non-repudiable asymmetric proof
252    signatures where the verifier has only the public key.
253    """
254    policy_hash: str          # "sha256:<hex>"
255    context_hash: str         # "sha256:<hex>"
256    rule_index: int           # -1 = DEFAULT_DENY
257    matched_condition: str    # "" for DEFAULT_DENY
258    verdict: TarlVerdict
259    evaluated_at: str         # ISO-8601 UTC
260    trace: list[dict]         # [{rule_index, condition, matched}, ...]
261    signature: str            # "hmac-sha256:<hex>", "ed25519:<hex>", or ""
262    key_id: str               # signing key identifier or ""
263
264    def canonical_bytes(self) -> bytes:
265        """Deterministic serialisation used for signing and verification."""
266        import json
267        data = {
268            "policy_hash": self.policy_hash,
269            "context_hash": self.context_hash,
270            "rule_index": self.rule_index,
271            "matched_condition": self.matched_condition,
272            "verdict": self.verdict.value,
273            "evaluated_at": self.evaluated_at,
274            "trace": self.trace,
275        }
276        return json.dumps(
277            data, sort_keys=True, separators=(',', ':')
278        ).encode('utf-8')
279
280    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
281        return {
282            "policy_hash": self.policy_hash,
283            "context_hash": self.context_hash,
284            "rule_index": self.rule_index,
285            "matched_condition": self.matched_condition,
286            "verdict": self.verdict.value,
287            "evaluated_at": self.evaluated_at,
288            "trace": list(self.trace),
289            "signature": self.signature,
290            "key_id": self.key_id,
291        }
292
293    def to_json(self) -> str:
294        import json
295        return json.dumps(self.to_dict(), indent=2)
296
297    @classmethod
298    def from_dict(cls, d: dict) -> TarlProof:
299        return cls(
300            policy_hash=d["policy_hash"],
301            context_hash=d["context_hash"],
302            rule_index=d["rule_index"],
303            matched_condition=d.get("matched_condition", ""),
304            verdict=TarlVerdict(d["verdict"]),
305            evaluated_at=d["evaluated_at"],
306            trace=d.get("trace", []),
307            signature=d.get("signature", ""),
308            key_id=d.get("key_id", ""),
309        )
310
311    @classmethod
312    def from_json(cls, s: str) -> TarlProof:
313        import json
314        return cls.from_dict(json.loads(s))

Integrity certificate of a policy evaluation.

Π = (H(P), H(c), k, v, T, σ) H(P) — SHA-256 of exact policy source used H(c) — SHA-256 of canonical context snapshot k — matched rule index (-1 = DEFAULT_DENY) v — final verdict T — evaluation trace: [{rule_index, condition, matched}, ...] σ — optional HMAC-SHA256 tag or Ed25519 signature over the canonical encoding

Proofs are unsigned by default. HMAC-SHA256 remains available as a symmetric compatibility mode: it is forgeable by any party that holds the key, including a verifier. Use Ed25519 for non-repudiable asymmetric proof signatures where the verifier has only the public key.

TarlProof( policy_hash: str, context_hash: str, rule_index: int, matched_condition: str, verdict: TarlVerdict, evaluated_at: str, trace: list[dict], signature: str, key_id: str)
policy_hash: str
context_hash: str
rule_index: int
matched_condition: str
verdict: TarlVerdict
evaluated_at: str
trace: list[dict]
signature: str
key_id: str
def canonical_bytes(self) -> bytes: 
264    def canonical_bytes(self) -> bytes:
265        """Deterministic serialisation used for signing and verification."""
266        import json
267        data = {
268            "policy_hash": self.policy_hash,
269            "context_hash": self.context_hash,
270            "rule_index": self.rule_index,
271            "matched_condition": self.matched_condition,
272            "verdict": self.verdict.value,
273            "evaluated_at": self.evaluated_at,
274            "trace": self.trace,
275        }
276        return json.dumps(
277            data, sort_keys=True, separators=(',', ':')
278        ).encode('utf-8')

Deterministic serialisation used for signing and verification.

def to_dict(self) -> dict:

281        return {

283            "context_hash": self.context_hash,
284            "rule_index": self.rule_index,
285            "matched_condition": self.matched_condition,

def to_json(self) -> str: 
293    def to_json(self) -> str:
294        import json
295        return json.dumps(self.to_dict(), indent=2)
@classmethod
def from_dict(cls, d: dict) -> TarlProof: 
297    @classmethod
298    def from_dict(cls, d: dict) -> TarlProof:
299        return cls(
300            policy_hash=d["policy_hash"],
301            context_hash=d["context_hash"],
302            rule_index=d["rule_index"],
303            matched_condition=d.get("matched_condition", ""),
304            verdict=TarlVerdict(d["verdict"]),
305            evaluated_at=d["evaluated_at"],
306            trace=d.get("trace", []),
307            signature=d.get("signature", ""),
308            key_id=d.get("key_id", ""),
309        )
@classmethod
def from_json(cls, s: str) -> TarlProof: 
311    @classmethod
312    def from_json(cls, s: str) -> TarlProof:
313        import json
314        return cls.from_dict(json.loads(s))
class CompositionOp(enum.StrEnum): 
69class CompositionOp(StrEnum):
70    """How a child policy composes with its parent."""
71    EXTENDS = "EXTENDS"
72    RESTRICTS = "RESTRICTS"
73
74    def __str__(self) -> str:
75        return self.value

How a child policy composes with its parent.

EXTENDS = <CompositionOp.EXTENDS: 'EXTENDS'>
RESTRICTS = <CompositionOp.RESTRICTS: 'RESTRICTS'>
class SetOp(enum.StrEnum): 
78class SetOp(StrEnum):
79    """How policies in a policy_set group are combined."""
80    UNION = "UNION"
81    INTERSECT = "INTERSECT"
82    MAJORITY = "MAJORITY"
83
84    def __str__(self) -> str:
85        return self.value

How policies in a policy_set group are combined.

UNION = <SetOp.UNION: 'UNION'>
INTERSECT = <SetOp.INTERSECT: 'INTERSECT'>
MAJORITY = <SetOp.MAJORITY: 'MAJORITY'>
DEFAULT_DENY = TarlDecision(verdict=<TarlVerdict.DENY: 'DENY'>, reason='default-deny: no rule matched', rule_index=-1, matched_rule=None, expires_at=None)
class TarlRuntime: 
 70class TarlRuntime:
 71    """
 72    TARL policy runtime:
 73    - LRU decision cache (128 entries)
 74    - ThreadPoolExecutor for parallel rule evaluation
 75    - Adaptive ordering (most-frequently-matched rules evaluated first)
 76    - Dynamic source registry for source:name condition references
 77    """
 78
 79    def __init__(
 80        self,
 81        policy: TarlPolicy | None = None,
 82        max_workers: int = 4,
 83    ):
 84        self.policy = policy or TarlPolicy()
 85        self.cache = LRUCache(maxsize=128)
 86        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_workers)
 87        self._hit_counts: dict = {}
 88        self._throw_counts: dict = {}   # rule_index -> number of evaluation exceptions
 89        self._sources: dict = {}
 90        self._signing_keys: dict = {}   # key_id -> bytes (HMAC secrets)
 91        self._ed25519_signing_keys: dict = {}  # key_id -> Ed25519PrivateKey
 92        self._signing_key_id: str = ""  # active key
 93        self._signing_alg: str = ""     # "hmac-sha256" or "ed25519"
 94        self._archive = None            # TarlAuditArchive | None
 95        self._context_schema = None     # ContextSchema | None
 96        self._require_audit = False     # fail closed if audit cannot persist
 97        # Trusted time source for temporal checks; None => host clock.
 98        self._clock: Callable[[], datetime.datetime | None] | None = None
 99
100    def set_clock(self, clock) -> "TarlRuntime":
101        """Use a trusted time source for temporal-policy checks instead of the
102        host clock. ``clock`` is a zero-arg callable returning a timezone-aware
103        ``datetime`` (typically obtained by verifying a signed-time assertion via
104        ``utf.tarl.clock.TrustedClock``). A spoofed system clock then cannot
105        satisfy a temporal window (C043). Returns self."""
106        self._clock = clock
107        return self
108
109    def _now(self):
110        return self._clock() if self._clock is not None else None
111
112    def set_require_audit(self, required: bool = True) -> "TarlRuntime":
113        """When True and an audit archive is attached, a failure to persist a
114        proof downgrades the decision to a fail-closed DENY (C038): execution
115        cannot proceed if the required audit record could not be written."""
116        self._require_audit = required
117        return self
118
119    def _persist(
120        self,
121        policy: TarlPolicy,
122        ctx: dict,
123        decision: TarlDecision,
124        proof: TarlProof,
125    ) -> tuple[TarlDecision, TarlProof]:
126        """Store the proof; on a persistence failure, fail closed when audit is
127        required. Returns the (possibly downgraded) ``(decision, proof)``."""
128        if self._archive is None:
129            return decision, proof
130        try:
131            self._archive.store(proof, expires_at=decision.expires_at)
132            return decision, proof
133        except Exception as exc:  # disk full, DoS on the audit sink, etc.
134            if not self._require_audit:
135                return decision, proof
136            denied = TarlDecision(
137                verdict=TarlVerdict.DENY,
138                reason=f"fail-closed: required audit could not be persisted: {exc}",
139            )
140            denied_proof = self._generate_proof(
141                policy, ctx, denied, -1,
142                [{"kind": "audit-fail", "matched": False, "reason": str(exc)}])
143            return denied, denied_proof
144
145    # ── Context schema ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
146
147    def set_context_schema(self, schema) -> "TarlRuntime":
148        """Attach a ``utf.tarl.schema.ContextSchema``. When set, every context is
149        validated before any rule runs; a missing required field or a
150        type-confused value short-circuits to the schema's fail-closed verdict
151        (DENY by default) instead of silently matching a permissive later rule.
152        Returns self for chaining."""
153        self._context_schema = schema
154        return self
155
156    def _schema_decision(self, context: dict) -> "TarlDecision | None":
157        """Return a fail-closed decision when ``context`` violates the schema,
158        else None."""
159        if self._context_schema is None:
160            return None
161        violations = self._context_schema.validate(context)
162        if not violations:
163            return None
164        return TarlDecision(
165            verdict=self._context_schema.on_violation,
166            reason="context schema violation: " + "; ".join(violations),
167        )
168
169    # ── Audit archive ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
170
171    def set_archive(self, archive) -> "TarlRuntime":
172        """
173        Attach a TarlAuditArchive.  Proofs generated by evaluate_with_proof()
174        are stored automatically.  Returns self for chaining.
175        """
176        self._archive = archive
177        return self
178
179    # ── Signing key registry ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
180
181    def set_signing_key(
182        self, key_id: str, secret: bytes
183    ) -> "TarlRuntime":
184        """
185        Register an HMAC-SHA256 signing key for proof generation.
186        The most recently set key becomes the active key.
187        Returns self for chaining.
188        """
189        self._signing_keys[key_id] = secret
190        self._signing_key_id = key_id
191        self._signing_alg = "hmac-sha256"
192        return self
193
194    def set_ed25519_signing_key(
195        self, key_id: str, private_key: bytes | Ed25519PrivateKey
196    ) -> "TarlRuntime":
197        """
198        Register an Ed25519 private key for proof generation.
199
200        ``private_key`` may be a cryptography Ed25519PrivateKey or the raw
201        32-byte private seed accepted by Ed25519PrivateKey.from_private_bytes().
202        The most recently set key becomes the active key.
203        """
204        if isinstance(private_key, Ed25519PrivateKey):
205            key = private_key
206        else:
207            key = Ed25519PrivateKey.from_private_bytes(private_key)
208        self._ed25519_signing_keys[key_id] = key
209        self._signing_key_id = key_id
210        self._signing_alg = "ed25519"
211        return self
212
213    # ── Source registry ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
214
215    def register_source(
216        self, name: str, provider
217    ) -> "TarlRuntime":
218        """
219        Bind a data provider to source:<name> condition references.
220
221        provider — a list/set (static) or a zero-arg callable that
222                   returns a list/set each time it is called.
223        Returns self for chaining.
224        """
225        self._sources[name] = provider
226        return self
227
228    def _inject_sources(self, context: dict) -> dict:
229        """Resolve all registered sources and inject into a context copy."""
230        if not self._sources:
231            return context
232        ctx = dict(context)
233        for name, provider in self._sources.items():
234            try:
235                value = provider() if callable(provider) else provider
236            except Exception:
237                value = []
238            ctx[f"source:{name}"] = value
239        return ctx
240
241    # ── Policy management ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
242
243    def throw_stats(self) -> dict:
244        """
245        Return throw counts per rule index.
246
247        **What the number means:**
248        Counts reflect distinct contexts that missed the cache and threw, not
249        total call frequency.  A rule that throws on one bad context evaluated
250        a thousand times shows throw_count == 1, not 1000, because cached
251        results bypass rule evaluation entirely.  The stat answers "how many
252        distinct inputs break this rule," not "how often throwing happens in
253        production."  Do not use it to gauge live-traffic blast radius without
254        accounting for cache hit rate.
255
256        **Predicates:**
257        - throw_count > 0 and hit_count == 0 → dead-by-exception: the rule
258          has never matched cleanly and throws on every context seen so far.
259        - throw_count > 0 and hit_count > 0 → partial-throw: the rule matches
260          on some inputs but throws on others.  hit_count > 0 does not mean
261          healthy; partial-throw is a distinct broken state.
262        """
263        return dict(self._throw_counts)
264
265    def set_policy(self, new_policy: TarlPolicy):
266        """Replace the active policy and reset the cache and hit counts."""
267        self.policy = new_policy
268        self.cache.clear()
269        self._hit_counts = dict.fromkeys(range(len(new_policy.rules)), 0)
270        self._throw_counts = {}
271
272    # ── Evaluation ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
273
274    def evaluate(
275        self,
276        context: dict,
277        policy_text: str | None = None,
278    ) -> TarlDecision:
279        """
280        Evaluate the active policy (or policy_text if supplied) against
281        context. Sources are resolved before evaluation. Returns the
282        first matching rule's verdict, or DEFAULT_DENY.
283        """
284        ctx = self._inject_sources(context)
285
286        # Context schema validation fails closed before any rule evaluation.
287        schema_decision = self._schema_decision(ctx)
288        if schema_decision is not None:
289            return schema_decision
290
291        cache_key = str(sorted(ctx.items()))
292
293        if policy_text is not None:
294            policy = PolicyParser.parse(policy_text)
295        else:
296            policy = self.policy
297
298        # Phase 5: enforce temporal window before any rule evaluation
299        temporal = _check_policy_temporal(policy, now=self._now())
300        if temporal is not None:
301            return temporal
302
303        if not policy.rules:
304            return DEFAULT_DENY
305
306        # Policies with temporal constraints must not be cached — the window
307        # check and expires_at timestamps would be stale on subsequent calls.
308        use_cache = not _is_temporally_constrained(policy)
309
310        if use_cache:
311            cached = self.cache.get(cache_key)
312            if cached is not None:
313                return cached
314
315        ordered_indices = sorted(
316            range(len(policy.rules)),
317            key=lambda i: self._hit_counts.get(i, 0),
318            reverse=True,
319        )
320
321        futures_by_idx = {}
322        for idx in ordered_indices:
323            rule = policy.rules[idx]
324            future = self.executor.submit(
325                self._evaluate_rule, rule, ctx
326            )
327            futures_by_idx[idx] = (future, rule)
328
329        results = {}
330        for idx, (future, rule) in futures_by_idx.items():
331            try:
332                matched, decision, threw = future.result()
333                results[idx] = (matched, decision, rule, threw)
334            except Exception:
335                results[idx] = (
336                    False,
337                    TarlDecision(
338                        verdict=TarlVerdict.DENY,
339                        reason="Evaluation error",
340                    ),
341                    rule,
342                    True,
343                )
344
345        # Iterate in POLICY ORDER (not hit-count order) to honour
346        # first-match-wins semantics. Adaptive ordering affects which
347        # futures are submitted first, not which result wins.
348        for idx in range(len(policy.rules)):
349            matched, decision, rule, threw = results[idx]
350            if threw:
351                self._throw_counts[idx] = (
352                    self._throw_counts.get(idx, 0) + 1
353                )
354            if matched:
355                self._hit_counts[idx] = (
356                    self._hit_counts.get(idx, 0) + 1
357                )
358                expires_at = None
359                if rule.duration_seconds:
360                    expires_at = (
361                        datetime.datetime.now(datetime.UTC)
362                        + datetime.timedelta(seconds=rule.duration_seconds)
363                    ).isoformat(timespec="seconds")
364                result = TarlDecision(
365                    verdict=decision.verdict,
366                    reason=decision.reason or f"Rule matched: {rule}",
367                    rule_index=idx,
368                    matched_rule=str(rule),
369                    expires_at=expires_at,
370                )
371                if use_cache:
372                    self.cache.put(cache_key, result)
373                return result
374
375        if use_cache:
376            self.cache.put(cache_key, DEFAULT_DENY)
377        return DEFAULT_DENY
378
379    def _evaluate_rule(
380        self, rule: TarlRule, context: dict
381    ) -> tuple:
382        """
383        Evaluate one rule. Returns (matched: bool, TarlDecision, threw: bool).
384
385        threw=True when the condition raised an exception.  The rule is
386        treated as non-matching in either case (fail-safe), but callers
387        increment _throw_counts so persistent exception behaviour is visible
388        via throw_stats().
389        """
390        try:
391            tokens = PolicyParser._tokenize(rule.condition)
392            matched = SafeExpr.evaluate(tokens, context)
393            if matched:
394                return True, TarlDecision(
395                    verdict=rule.verdict,
396                    reason=f"Condition '{rule.condition}' matched",
397                ), False
398            return False, TarlDecision(
399                verdict=rule.verdict,
400                reason="Condition did not match",
401            ), False
402        except Exception as exc:
403            return False, TarlDecision(
404                verdict=rule.verdict,
405                reason=f"Evaluation error: {exc}",
406            ), True
407
408    # ── Proof-carrying evaluation ─────────────────────────────────────────────
409
410    def evaluate_with_proof(
411        self,
412        context: dict,
413        policy_text: str | None = None,
414    ) -> tuple[TarlDecision, TarlProof]:
415        """
416        Evaluate sequentially, recording a full evaluation trace, then sign.
417
418        Returns (TarlDecision, TarlProof). The proof contains:
419          - SHA-256 hash of policy source
420          - SHA-256 hash of canonical context
421          - Per-rule trace up to (and including) the first match
422          - HMAC-SHA256 or Ed25519 signature if a signing key is registered
423        """
424        ctx = self._inject_sources(context)
425        if policy_text is not None:
426            policy = PolicyParser.parse(policy_text)
427        else:
428            policy = self.policy
429
430        # Context schema validation fails closed before any rule evaluation,
431        # carrying a proof that records which fields were missing or mistyped.
432        schema_decision = self._schema_decision(ctx)
433        if schema_decision is not None:
434            trace = [{"kind": "schema-violation", "matched": False,
435                      "reason": schema_decision.reason}]
436            proof = self._generate_proof(policy, ctx, schema_decision, -1, trace)
437            return self._persist(policy, ctx, schema_decision, proof)
438
439        # Phase 5: temporal window check — return early with proof if outside window
440        temporal = _check_policy_temporal(policy, now=self._now())
441        if temporal is not None:
442            proof = self._generate_proof(policy, ctx, temporal, -1, [])
443            return self._persist(policy, ctx, temporal, proof)
444
445        trace = []
446        decision = DEFAULT_DENY
447        matched_idx = -1
448
449        for i, rule in enumerate(policy.rules):
450            matched, rule_dec, threw = self._evaluate_rule(rule, ctx)
451            if threw:
452                self._throw_counts[i] = (
453                    self._throw_counts.get(i, 0) + 1
454                )
455            trace.append({
456                "rule_index": i,
457                "condition": rule.condition,
458                "matched": matched,
459            })
460            if matched:
461                matched_idx = i
462                expires_at = None
463                if rule.duration_seconds:
464                    expires_at = (
465                        datetime.datetime.now(datetime.UTC)
466                        + datetime.timedelta(seconds=rule.duration_seconds)
467                    ).isoformat(timespec="seconds")
468                decision = TarlDecision(
469                    verdict=rule_dec.verdict,
470                    reason=f"Condition '{rule.condition}' matched",
471                    rule_index=i,
472                    matched_rule=str(rule),
473                    expires_at=expires_at,
474                )
475                break
476
477        proof = self._generate_proof(policy, ctx, decision, matched_idx, trace)
478        return self._persist(policy, ctx, decision, proof)
479
480    def _generate_proof(
481        self,
482        policy: TarlPolicy,
483        context: dict,
484        decision: TarlDecision,
485        matched_idx: int,
486        trace: list,
487    ) -> TarlProof:
488        policy_hash = "sha256:" + hashlib.sha256(
489            policy.source.encode("utf-8")
490        ).hexdigest()
491        ctx_bytes = json.dumps(
492            context, sort_keys=True, default=str, separators=(",", ":")
493        ).encode("utf-8")
494        context_hash = "sha256:" + hashlib.sha256(ctx_bytes).hexdigest()
495        evaluated_at = (
496            datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat(timespec="seconds") + "Z"
497        )
498        matched_condition = (
499            policy.rules[matched_idx].condition if matched_idx >= 0 else ""
500        )
501        proof = TarlProof(
502            policy_hash=policy_hash,
503            context_hash=context_hash,
504            rule_index=matched_idx,
505            matched_condition=matched_condition,
506            verdict=decision.verdict,
507            evaluated_at=evaluated_at,
508            trace=trace,
509            signature="",
510            key_id="",
511        )
512        if self._signing_key_id and self._signing_alg == "hmac-sha256":
513            secret = self._signing_keys.get(self._signing_key_id)
514            if secret is not None:
515                sig_hex = hmac.new(
516                    secret, proof.canonical_bytes(), hashlib.sha256
517                ).hexdigest()
518                proof.signature = f"hmac-sha256:{sig_hex}"
519                proof.key_id = self._signing_key_id
520        elif self._signing_key_id and self._signing_alg == "ed25519":
521            key = self._ed25519_signing_keys.get(self._signing_key_id)
522            if key is not None:
523                sig_hex = key.sign(proof.canonical_bytes()).hex()
524                proof.signature = f"ed25519:{sig_hex}"
525                proof.key_id = self._signing_key_id
526        return proof
527
528    def shutdown(self):
529        """Clean up the thread pool."""
530        self.executor.shutdown(wait=False)

TARL policy runtime:

  • LRU decision cache (128 entries)
  • ThreadPoolExecutor for parallel rule evaluation
  • Adaptive ordering (most-frequently-matched rules evaluated first)
  • Dynamic source registry for source:name condition references
TarlRuntime(policy: TarlPolicy | None = None, max_workers: int = 4) 
79    def __init__(
80        self,
81        policy: TarlPolicy | None = None,
82        max_workers: int = 4,
83    ):
84        self.policy = policy or TarlPolicy()
85        self.cache = LRUCache(maxsize=128)
86        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_workers)
87        self._hit_counts: dict = {}
88        self._throw_counts: dict = {}   # rule_index -> number of evaluation exceptions
89        self._sources: dict = {}
90        self._signing_keys: dict = {}   # key_id -> bytes (HMAC secrets)
91        self._ed25519_signing_keys: dict = {}  # key_id -> Ed25519PrivateKey
92        self._signing_key_id: str = ""  # active key
93        self._signing_alg: str = ""     # "hmac-sha256" or "ed25519"
94        self._archive = None            # TarlAuditArchive | None
95        self._context_schema = None     # ContextSchema | None
96        self._require_audit = False     # fail closed if audit cannot persist
97        # Trusted time source for temporal checks; None => host clock.
98        self._clock: Callable[[], datetime.datetime | None] | None = None
policy
cache
executor
def set_clock(self, clock) -> TarlRuntime: 
100    def set_clock(self, clock) -> "TarlRuntime":
101        """Use a trusted time source for temporal-policy checks instead of the
102        host clock. ``clock`` is a zero-arg callable returning a timezone-aware
103        ``datetime`` (typically obtained by verifying a signed-time assertion via
104        ``utf.tarl.clock.TrustedClock``). A spoofed system clock then cannot
105        satisfy a temporal window (C043). Returns self."""
106        self._clock = clock
107        return self

Use a trusted time source for temporal-policy checks instead of the host clock. clock is a zero-arg callable returning a timezone-aware datetime (typically obtained by verifying a signed-time assertion via utf.tarl.clock.TrustedClock). A spoofed system clock then cannot satisfy a temporal window (C043). Returns self.

def set_require_audit(self, required: bool = True) -> TarlRuntime: 
112    def set_require_audit(self, required: bool = True) -> "TarlRuntime":
113        """When True and an audit archive is attached, a failure to persist a
114        proof downgrades the decision to a fail-closed DENY (C038): execution
115        cannot proceed if the required audit record could not be written."""
116        self._require_audit = required
117        return self

When True and an audit archive is attached, a failure to persist a proof downgrades the decision to a fail-closed DENY (C038): execution cannot proceed if the required audit record could not be written.

def set_context_schema(self, schema) -> TarlRuntime: 
147    def set_context_schema(self, schema) -> "TarlRuntime":
148        """Attach a ``utf.tarl.schema.ContextSchema``. When set, every context is
149        validated before any rule runs; a missing required field or a
150        type-confused value short-circuits to the schema's fail-closed verdict
151        (DENY by default) instead of silently matching a permissive later rule.
152        Returns self for chaining."""
153        self._context_schema = schema
154        return self

Attach a utf.tarl.schema.ContextSchema. When set, every context is validated before any rule runs; a missing required field or a type-confused value short-circuits to the schema's fail-closed verdict (DENY by default) instead of silently matching a permissive later rule. Returns self for chaining.

def set_archive(self, archive) -> TarlRuntime: 
171    def set_archive(self, archive) -> "TarlRuntime":
172        """
173        Attach a TarlAuditArchive.  Proofs generated by evaluate_with_proof()
174        are stored automatically.  Returns self for chaining.
175        """
176        self._archive = archive
177        return self

Attach a TarlAuditArchive. Proofs generated by evaluate_with_proof() are stored automatically. Returns self for chaining.

def set_signing_key(self, key_id: str, secret: bytes) -> TarlRuntime: 
181    def set_signing_key(
182        self, key_id: str, secret: bytes
183    ) -> "TarlRuntime":
184        """
185        Register an HMAC-SHA256 signing key for proof generation.
186        The most recently set key becomes the active key.
187        Returns self for chaining.
188        """
189        self._signing_keys[key_id] = secret
190        self._signing_key_id = key_id
191        self._signing_alg = "hmac-sha256"
192        return self

Register an HMAC-SHA256 signing key for proof generation. The most recently set key becomes the active key. Returns self for chaining.

def set_ed25519_signing_key( self, key_id: str, private_key: bytes | cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.ed25519.Ed25519PrivateKey) -> TarlRuntime: 
194    def set_ed25519_signing_key(
195        self, key_id: str, private_key: bytes | Ed25519PrivateKey
196    ) -> "TarlRuntime":
197        """
198        Register an Ed25519 private key for proof generation.
199
200        ``private_key`` may be a cryptography Ed25519PrivateKey or the raw
201        32-byte private seed accepted by Ed25519PrivateKey.from_private_bytes().
202        The most recently set key becomes the active key.
203        """
204        if isinstance(private_key, Ed25519PrivateKey):
205            key = private_key
206        else:
207            key = Ed25519PrivateKey.from_private_bytes(private_key)
208        self._ed25519_signing_keys[key_id] = key
209        self._signing_key_id = key_id
210        self._signing_alg = "ed25519"
211        return self

Register an Ed25519 private key for proof generation.

private_key may be a cryptography Ed25519PrivateKey or the raw 32-byte private seed accepted by Ed25519PrivateKey.from_private_bytes(). The most recently set key becomes the active key.

def register_source(self, name: str, provider) -> TarlRuntime: 
215    def register_source(
216        self, name: str, provider
217    ) -> "TarlRuntime":
218        """
219        Bind a data provider to source:<name> condition references.
220
221        provider — a list/set (static) or a zero-arg callable that
222                   returns a list/set each time it is called.
223        Returns self for chaining.
224        """
225        self._sources[name] = provider
226        return self

Bind a data provider to source: condition references.

provider — a list/set (static) or a zero-arg callable that returns a list/set each time it is called. Returns self for chaining.

def throw_stats(self) -> dict: 
243    def throw_stats(self) -> dict:
244        """
245        Return throw counts per rule index.
246
247        **What the number means:**
248        Counts reflect distinct contexts that missed the cache and threw, not
249        total call frequency.  A rule that throws on one bad context evaluated
250        a thousand times shows throw_count == 1, not 1000, because cached
251        results bypass rule evaluation entirely.  The stat answers "how many
252        distinct inputs break this rule," not "how often throwing happens in
253        production."  Do not use it to gauge live-traffic blast radius without
254        accounting for cache hit rate.
255
256        **Predicates:**
257        - throw_count > 0 and hit_count == 0 → dead-by-exception: the rule
258          has never matched cleanly and throws on every context seen so far.
259        - throw_count > 0 and hit_count > 0 → partial-throw: the rule matches
260          on some inputs but throws on others.  hit_count > 0 does not mean
261          healthy; partial-throw is a distinct broken state.
262        """
263        return dict(self._throw_counts)

Return throw counts per rule index.

What the number means: Counts reflect distinct contexts that missed the cache and threw, not total call frequency. A rule that throws on one bad context evaluated a thousand times shows throw_count == 1, not 1000, because cached results bypass rule evaluation entirely. The stat answers "how many distinct inputs break this rule," not "how often throwing happens in production." Do not use it to gauge live-traffic blast radius without accounting for cache hit rate.

Predicates:

  • throw_count > 0 and hit_count == 0 → dead-by-exception: the rule has never matched cleanly and throws on every context seen so far.
  • throw_count > 0 and hit_count > 0 → partial-throw: the rule matches on some inputs but throws on others. hit_count > 0 does not mean healthy; partial-throw is a distinct broken state.
def set_policy(self, new_policy: TarlPolicy): 
265    def set_policy(self, new_policy: TarlPolicy):
266        """Replace the active policy and reset the cache and hit counts."""
267        self.policy = new_policy
268        self.cache.clear()
269        self._hit_counts = dict.fromkeys(range(len(new_policy.rules)), 0)
270        self._throw_counts = {}

Replace the active policy and reset the cache and hit counts.

def evaluate( self, context: dict, policy_text: str | None = None) -> TarlDecision: 
274    def evaluate(
275        self,
276        context: dict,
277        policy_text: str | None = None,
278    ) -> TarlDecision:
279        """
280        Evaluate the active policy (or policy_text if supplied) against
281        context. Sources are resolved before evaluation. Returns the
282        first matching rule's verdict, or DEFAULT_DENY.
283        """
284        ctx = self._inject_sources(context)
285
286        # Context schema validation fails closed before any rule evaluation.
287        schema_decision = self._schema_decision(ctx)
288        if schema_decision is not None:
289            return schema_decision
290
291        cache_key = str(sorted(ctx.items()))
292
293        if policy_text is not None:
294            policy = PolicyParser.parse(policy_text)
295        else:
296            policy = self.policy
297
298        # Phase 5: enforce temporal window before any rule evaluation
299        temporal = _check_policy_temporal(policy, now=self._now())
300        if temporal is not None:
301            return temporal
302
303        if not policy.rules:
304            return DEFAULT_DENY
305
306        # Policies with temporal constraints must not be cached — the window
307        # check and expires_at timestamps would be stale on subsequent calls.
308        use_cache = not _is_temporally_constrained(policy)
309
310        if use_cache:
311            cached = self.cache.get(cache_key)
312            if cached is not None:
313                return cached
314
315        ordered_indices = sorted(
316            range(len(policy.rules)),
317            key=lambda i: self._hit_counts.get(i, 0),
318            reverse=True,
319        )
320
321        futures_by_idx = {}
322        for idx in ordered_indices:
323            rule = policy.rules[idx]
324            future = self.executor.submit(
325                self._evaluate_rule, rule, ctx
326            )
327            futures_by_idx[idx] = (future, rule)
328
329        results = {}
330        for idx, (future, rule) in futures_by_idx.items():
331            try:
332                matched, decision, threw = future.result()
333                results[idx] = (matched, decision, rule, threw)
334            except Exception:
335                results[idx] = (
336                    False,
337                    TarlDecision(
338                        verdict=TarlVerdict.DENY,
339                        reason="Evaluation error",
340                    ),
341                    rule,
342                    True,
343                )
344
345        # Iterate in POLICY ORDER (not hit-count order) to honour
346        # first-match-wins semantics. Adaptive ordering affects which
347        # futures are submitted first, not which result wins.
348        for idx in range(len(policy.rules)):
349            matched, decision, rule, threw = results[idx]
350            if threw:
351                self._throw_counts[idx] = (
352                    self._throw_counts.get(idx, 0) + 1
353                )
354            if matched:
355                self._hit_counts[idx] = (
356                    self._hit_counts.get(idx, 0) + 1
357                )
358                expires_at = None
359                if rule.duration_seconds:
360                    expires_at = (
361                        datetime.datetime.now(datetime.UTC)
362                        + datetime.timedelta(seconds=rule.duration_seconds)
363                    ).isoformat(timespec="seconds")
364                result = TarlDecision(
365                    verdict=decision.verdict,
366                    reason=decision.reason or f"Rule matched: {rule}",
367                    rule_index=idx,
368                    matched_rule=str(rule),
369                    expires_at=expires_at,
370                )
371                if use_cache:
372                    self.cache.put(cache_key, result)
373                return result
374
375        if use_cache:
376            self.cache.put(cache_key, DEFAULT_DENY)
377        return DEFAULT_DENY

Evaluate the active policy (or policy_text if supplied) against context. Sources are resolved before evaluation. Returns the first matching rule's verdict, or DEFAULT_DENY.

def evaluate_with_proof( self, context: dict, policy_text: str | None = None) -> tuple[TarlDecision, TarlProof]: 
410    def evaluate_with_proof(
411        self,
412        context: dict,
413        policy_text: str | None = None,
414    ) -> tuple[TarlDecision, TarlProof]:
415        """
416        Evaluate sequentially, recording a full evaluation trace, then sign.
417
418        Returns (TarlDecision, TarlProof). The proof contains:
419          - SHA-256 hash of policy source
420          - SHA-256 hash of canonical context
421          - Per-rule trace up to (and including) the first match
422          - HMAC-SHA256 or Ed25519 signature if a signing key is registered
423        """
424        ctx = self._inject_sources(context)
425        if policy_text is not None:
426            policy = PolicyParser.parse(policy_text)
427        else:
428            policy = self.policy
429
430        # Context schema validation fails closed before any rule evaluation,
431        # carrying a proof that records which fields were missing or mistyped.
432        schema_decision = self._schema_decision(ctx)
433        if schema_decision is not None:
434            trace = [{"kind": "schema-violation", "matched": False,
435                      "reason": schema_decision.reason}]
436            proof = self._generate_proof(policy, ctx, schema_decision, -1, trace)
437            return self._persist(policy, ctx, schema_decision, proof)
438
439        # Phase 5: temporal window check — return early with proof if outside window
440        temporal = _check_policy_temporal(policy, now=self._now())
441        if temporal is not None:
442            proof = self._generate_proof(policy, ctx, temporal, -1, [])
443            return self._persist(policy, ctx, temporal, proof)
444
445        trace = []
446        decision = DEFAULT_DENY
447        matched_idx = -1
448
449        for i, rule in enumerate(policy.rules):
450            matched, rule_dec, threw = self._evaluate_rule(rule, ctx)
451            if threw:
452                self._throw_counts[i] = (
453                    self._throw_counts.get(i, 0) + 1
454                )
455            trace.append({
456                "rule_index": i,
457                "condition": rule.condition,
458                "matched": matched,
459            })
460            if matched:
461                matched_idx = i
462                expires_at = None
463                if rule.duration_seconds:
464                    expires_at = (
465                        datetime.datetime.now(datetime.UTC)
466                        + datetime.timedelta(seconds=rule.duration_seconds)
467                    ).isoformat(timespec="seconds")
468                decision = TarlDecision(
469                    verdict=rule_dec.verdict,
470                    reason=f"Condition '{rule.condition}' matched",
471                    rule_index=i,
472                    matched_rule=str(rule),
473                    expires_at=expires_at,
474                )
475                break
476
477        proof = self._generate_proof(policy, ctx, decision, matched_idx, trace)
478        return self._persist(policy, ctx, decision, proof)

Evaluate sequentially, recording a full evaluation trace, then sign.

Returns (TarlDecision, TarlProof). The proof contains:

  • SHA-256 hash of policy source
  • SHA-256 hash of canonical context
  • Per-rule trace up to (and including) the first match
  • HMAC-SHA256 or Ed25519 signature if a signing key is registered
def shutdown(self): 
528    def shutdown(self):
529        """Clean up the thread pool."""
530        self.executor.shutdown(wait=False)

Clean up the thread pool.

class PolicyComposer: 
 38class PolicyComposer:
 39    """
 40    Registry and evaluator for composed T.A.R.L. policies.
 41
 42    Phase 5: temporal window enforcement and policy succession (revert_to)
 43    are applied automatically before EXTENDS/RESTRICTS dispatch.
 44
 45    Usage:
 46        composer = PolicyComposer()
 47        composer.register(base_policy)
 48        composer.register(child_policy)   # child EXTENDS base
 49        decision = composer.evaluate("child", context)
 50    """
 51
 52    def __init__(self, base_path: str = "."):
 53        self._policies: dict[str, TarlPolicy] = {}
 54        self._sets: dict[str, TarlPolicySet] = {}
 55        self._base_path = base_path
 56
 57    # ── Registration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 58
 59    def register(self, policy: TarlPolicy) -> PolicyComposer:
 60        """Register a TarlPolicy by name. Returns self for chaining."""
 61        self._policies[policy.name] = policy
 62        return self
 63
 64    def register_set(self, policy_set: TarlPolicySet) -> PolicyComposer:
 65        """Register a TarlPolicySet by name. Returns self for chaining."""
 66        self._sets[policy_set.name] = policy_set
 67        return self
 68
 69    def register_from_text(
 70        self, text: str, name: str | None = None
 71    ) -> PolicyComposer:
 72        """
 73        Parse text and register all policies and policy_sets it contains.
 74        Returns self for chaining.
 75        """
 76        from utf.tarl.core import PolicyParser
 77
 78        items = PolicyParser.parse_all(text)
 79        for item in items:
 80            if isinstance(item, TarlPolicySet):
 81                self._sets[item.name] = item
 82            elif isinstance(item, TarlPolicy):
 83                if name and len(items) == 1 and item.name == "unnamed":
 84                    item.name = name
 85                self._policies[item.name] = item
 86        return self
 87
 88    def load_file(self, path: str) -> PolicyComposer:
 89        """
 90        Read a .tarl file and register everything it contains.
 91        Paths are resolved relative to self._base_path.
 92        Returns self for chaining.
 93        """
 94        full = (
 95            path if os.path.isabs(path)
 96            else os.path.join(self._base_path, path)
 97        )
 98        with open(full, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
 99            return self.register_from_text(fh.read())
100
101    def names(self) -> list[str]:
102        """All registered policy names."""
103        return list(self._policies.keys())
104
105    def set_names(self) -> list[str]:
106        """All registered policy_set names."""
107        return list(self._sets.keys())
108
109    # ── Public evaluation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
110
111    def evaluate(self, name: str, context: dict) -> TarlDecision:
112        """
113        Evaluate a named policy or policy_set against context.
114        Raises CompositionError if the name is not registered.
115        """
116        if name in self._sets:
117            return self._evaluate_set(name, context)
118        if name in self._policies:
119            return self._evaluate_policy(
120                self._policies[name], context, frozenset()
121            )
122        registered = sorted(self._policies) + sorted(self._sets)
123        raise CompositionError(
124            f"Unknown policy or policy_set: {name!r}. "
125            f"Registered: {registered}"
126        )
127
128    # ── Internal policy evaluation ────────────────────────────────────────────
129
130    def _evaluate_policy(
131        self,
132        policy: TarlPolicy,
133        context: dict,
134        chain: frozenset[str],
135    ) -> TarlDecision:
136        """Dispatch to the correct composition handler."""
137        from utf.tarl.core import _check_policy_temporal, evaluate_policy
138
139        # Phase 5: temporal window check before any rule evaluation.
140        # If the policy has a revert_to target registered in this composer,
141        # evaluate that policy instead (succession). Otherwise return the
142        # on_expiry verdict (or ESCALATE).
143        temporal = _check_policy_temporal(policy)
144        if temporal is not None:
145            if policy.revert_to and policy.revert_to in self._policies:
146                # Guard against succession cycles (A→B→A) using the same
147                # chain mechanism as EXTENDS/RESTRICTS.
148                self._check_cycle(policy.name, chain)
149                revert_policy = self._policies[policy.revert_to]
150                return self._evaluate_policy(
151                    revert_policy, context, chain | {policy.name}
152                )
153            return temporal
154
155        # Pre-evaluate INCLUDE directives, inject into context copy
156        ctx = dict(context)
157        if policy.includes:
158            ctx = self._inject_includes(policy.includes, ctx, chain)
159
160        if policy.composition == CompositionOp.EXTENDS:
161            return self._evaluate_extends(policy, ctx, chain)
162        if policy.composition == CompositionOp.RESTRICTS:
163            return self._evaluate_restricts(policy, ctx, chain)
164
165        return evaluate_policy(ctx, policy=policy)
166
167    def _evaluate_extends(
168        self,
169        policy: TarlPolicy,
170        context: dict,
171        chain: frozenset[str],
172    ) -> TarlDecision:
173        """
174        EXTENDS semantics:
175          1. Evaluate child rules only.
176          2. If a child rule matched → return that decision.
177          3. If STOP is set → return DEFAULT_DENY (no parent fallthrough).
178          4. Otherwise → fall through to parent rules.
179        """
180        from utf.tarl.core import evaluate_policy
181
182        self._check_cycle(policy.name, chain)
183
184        child_only = TarlPolicy(
185            rules=list(policy.rules),
186            name=policy.name,
187        )
188        decision = evaluate_policy(context, policy=child_only)
189
190        if decision.rule_index >= 0:
191            return decision
192
193        if policy.has_stop:
194            return DEFAULT_DENY
195
196        if not policy.parent:
197            return DEFAULT_DENY
198
199        parent = self._require(policy.parent, policy.name)
200        return self._evaluate_policy(
201            parent, context, chain | {policy.name}
202        )
203
204    def _evaluate_restricts(
205        self,
206        policy: TarlPolicy,
207        context: dict,
208        chain: frozenset[str],
209    ) -> TarlDecision:
210        """
211        RESTRICTS semantics:
212          Evaluate child and parent independently.
213          Final verdict = meet(child, parent) — stricter wins.
214        """
215        from utf.tarl.core import evaluate_policy
216
217        self._check_cycle(policy.name, chain)
218
219        child_only = TarlPolicy(
220            rules=list(policy.rules),
221            name=policy.name,
222        )
223        child_dec = evaluate_policy(context, policy=child_only)
224
225        parent = self._require(policy.parent, policy.name)
226        parent_dec = self._evaluate_policy(
227            parent, context, chain | {policy.name}
228        )
229
230        meet = TarlVerdict.meet(child_dec.verdict, parent_dec.verdict)
231        src = child_dec if meet == child_dec.verdict else parent_dec
232        return TarlDecision(
233            verdict=meet,
234            reason=(
235                f"RESTRICTS({policy.parent}): "
236                f"child={child_dec.verdict.value}, "
237                f"parent={parent_dec.verdict.value}"
238            ),
239            rule_index=src.rule_index,
240            matched_rule=src.matched_rule,
241        )
242
243    def _inject_includes(
244        self,
245        refs: list[TarlPolicyRef],
246        context: dict,
247        chain: frozenset[str],
248    ) -> dict:
249        """
250        Pre-evaluate each INCLUDE'd policy and inject its verdict into ctx.
251
252        Injects two keys per include:
253          ctx["<alias>"] = {"verdict": "<VERDICT>", "rule_index": <int>}
254          ctx["<alias>.verdict"] = "<VERDICT>"
255        """
256        ctx = dict(context)
257        for ref in refs:
258            alias = ref.alias or ref.name
259
260            # Resolve file-based includes
261            policy_name = ref.name
262            if ref.is_file:
263                stem = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(ref.name))[0]
264                if stem not in self._policies:
265                    try:
266                        self.load_file(ref.name)
267                    except OSError:
268                        ctx[alias] = {"verdict": "DENY", "rule_index": -1}
269                        ctx[f"{alias}.verdict"] = "DENY"
270                        continue
271                policy_name = stem
272
273            if policy_name not in self._policies:
274                ctx[alias] = {"verdict": "DENY", "rule_index": -1}
275                ctx[f"{alias}.verdict"] = "DENY"
276                continue
277
278            inc_policy = self._policies[policy_name]
279            inc_dec = self._evaluate_policy(inc_policy, ctx, chain)
280            ctx[alias] = {
281                "verdict": inc_dec.verdict.value,
282                "rule_index": inc_dec.rule_index,
283            }
284            ctx[f"{alias}.verdict"] = inc_dec.verdict.value
285
286        return ctx
287
288    # ── Policy-set evaluation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
289
290    def _evaluate_set(self, name: str, context: dict) -> TarlDecision:
291        """
292        policy_set evaluation:
293          Each group produces a verdict via its SetOp.
294          The final verdict is the meet (∧) of all group verdicts.
295          If no groups are defined, the set's default_verdict is returned.
296        """
297        ps = self._sets[name]
298
299        if not ps.groups:
300            return TarlDecision(
301                verdict=ps.default_verdict,
302                reason=f"policy_set {name}: no groups defined",
303            )
304
305        group_verdicts: list[TarlVerdict] = []
306        group_summaries: list[str] = []
307
308        for op, policy_names in ps.groups:
309            dec = self._evaluate_group(op, policy_names, context)
310            group_verdicts.append(dec.verdict)
311            group_summaries.append(f"{op.value}={dec.verdict.value}")
312
313        final = group_verdicts[0]
314        for v in group_verdicts[1:]:
315            final = TarlVerdict.meet(final, v)
316
317        return TarlDecision(
318            verdict=final,
319            reason=f"policy_set {name}: " + ", ".join(group_summaries),
320        )
321
322    def _evaluate_group(
323        self,
324        op: SetOp,
325        policy_names: list[str],
326        context: dict,
327    ) -> TarlDecision:
328        """Evaluate a single combine group using the given operator."""
329        verdicts: list[TarlVerdict] = []
330        for pname in policy_names:
331            if pname in self._policies:
332                dec = self._evaluate_policy(
333                    self._policies[pname], context, frozenset()
334                )
335                verdicts.append(dec.verdict)
336            else:
337                verdicts.append(TarlVerdict.DENY)
338
339        if not verdicts:
340            return TarlDecision(
341                verdict=TarlVerdict.DENY,
342                reason=f"{op.value}: empty group",
343            )
344
345        if op == SetOp.UNION:
346            result = verdicts[0]
347            for v in verdicts[1:]:
348                result = TarlVerdict.join(result, v)
349        elif op == SetOp.INTERSECT:
350            result = verdicts[0]
351            for v in verdicts[1:]:
352                result = TarlVerdict.meet(result, v)
353        elif op == SetOp.MAJORITY:
354            allow_count = sum(
355                1 for v in verdicts if v == TarlVerdict.ALLOW
356            )
357            result = (
358                TarlVerdict.ALLOW
359                if allow_count > len(verdicts) / 2
360                else TarlVerdict.DENY
361            )
362        else:
363            result = TarlVerdict.DENY
364
365        return TarlDecision(
366            verdict=result,
367            reason=(
368                f"{op.value}("
369                + ", ".join(v.value for v in verdicts)
370                + f") = {result.value}"
371            ),
372        )
373
374    # ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
375
376    def _require(self, name: str | None, from_name: str) -> TarlPolicy:
377        if name is None or name not in self._policies:
378            raise CompositionError(
379                f"Policy {from_name!r} references unknown policy "
380                f"{name!r}. Registered: {sorted(self._policies.keys())}"
381            )
382        return self._policies[name]
383
384    def _check_cycle(self, name: str, chain: frozenset[str]) -> None:
385        if name in chain:
386            path = " -> ".join(sorted(chain)) + f" -> {name}"
387            raise CompositionError(
388                f"Circular policy reference detected: {path}"
389            )

Registry and evaluator for composed T.A.R.L. policies.

Phase 5: temporal window enforcement and policy succession (revert_to) are applied automatically before EXTENDS/RESTRICTS dispatch.

Usage: composer = PolicyComposer() composer.register(base_policy) composer.register(child_policy) # child EXTENDS base decision = composer.evaluate("child", context)

PolicyComposer(base_path: str = '.') 
52    def __init__(self, base_path: str = "."):
53        self._policies: dict[str, TarlPolicy] = {}
54        self._sets: dict[str, TarlPolicySet] = {}
55        self._base_path = base_path
def register( self, policy: TarlPolicy) -> PolicyComposer: 
59    def register(self, policy: TarlPolicy) -> PolicyComposer:
60        """Register a TarlPolicy by name. Returns self for chaining."""
61        self._policies[policy.name] = policy
62        return self

Register a TarlPolicy by name. Returns self for chaining.

def register_set( self, policy_set: TarlPolicySet) -> PolicyComposer: 
64    def register_set(self, policy_set: TarlPolicySet) -> PolicyComposer:
65        """Register a TarlPolicySet by name. Returns self for chaining."""
66        self._sets[policy_set.name] = policy_set
67        return self

Register a TarlPolicySet by name. Returns self for chaining.

def register_from_text( self, text: str, name: str | None = None) -> PolicyComposer: 
69    def register_from_text(
70        self, text: str, name: str | None = None
71    ) -> PolicyComposer:
72        """
73        Parse text and register all policies and policy_sets it contains.
74        Returns self for chaining.
75        """
76        from utf.tarl.core import PolicyParser
77
78        items = PolicyParser.parse_all(text)
79        for item in items:
80            if isinstance(item, TarlPolicySet):
81                self._sets[item.name] = item
82            elif isinstance(item, TarlPolicy):
83                if name and len(items) == 1 and item.name == "unnamed":
84                    item.name = name
85                self._policies[item.name] = item
86        return self

Parse text and register all policies and policy_sets it contains. Returns self for chaining.

def load_file(self, path: str) -> PolicyComposer: 
88    def load_file(self, path: str) -> PolicyComposer:
89        """
90        Read a .tarl file and register everything it contains.
91        Paths are resolved relative to self._base_path.
92        Returns self for chaining.
93        """
94        full = (
95            path if os.path.isabs(path)
96            else os.path.join(self._base_path, path)
97        )
98        with open(full, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
99            return self.register_from_text(fh.read())

Read a .tarl file and register everything it contains. Paths are resolved relative to self._base_path. Returns self for chaining.

def names(self) -> list[str]: 
101    def names(self) -> list[str]:
102        """All registered policy names."""
103        return list(self._policies.keys())

All registered policy names.

def set_names(self) -> list[str]: 
105    def set_names(self) -> list[str]:
106        """All registered policy_set names."""
107        return list(self._sets.keys())

All registered policy_set names.

def evaluate(self, name: str, context: dict) -> TarlDecision: 
111    def evaluate(self, name: str, context: dict) -> TarlDecision:
112        """
113        Evaluate a named policy or policy_set against context.
114        Raises CompositionError if the name is not registered.
115        """
116        if name in self._sets:
117            return self._evaluate_set(name, context)
118        if name in self._policies:
119            return self._evaluate_policy(
120                self._policies[name], context, frozenset()
121            )
122        registered = sorted(self._policies) + sorted(self._sets)
123        raise CompositionError(
124            f"Unknown policy or policy_set: {name!r}. "
125            f"Registered: {registered}"
126        )

Evaluate a named policy or policy_set against context. Raises CompositionError if the name is not registered.

class CompositionError(builtins.Exception): 
34class CompositionError(Exception):
35    """Raised for invalid composition: unknown parent, circular reference."""

Raised for invalid composition: unknown parent, circular reference.

class PolicyAnalyzer: 
486class PolicyAnalyzer:
487    """
488    Static analysis engine for a single T.A.R.L. policy.
489
490    All methods return AnalysisResult.  When z3-solver is not installed every
491    result has available=False and carries an installation hint.
492    """
493
494    def __init__(self, policy: TarlPolicy) -> None:
495        self.policy = policy
496
497    def _translate(self) -> tuple:
498        tr = _ConditionToZ3()
499        tr.infer_types(self.policy.rules)
500        formulas = _build_formulas(tr, self.policy.rules)
501        return tr, formulas
502
503    # ── coverage ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
504
505    @_z3_serialized
506    def check_coverage(self) -> AnalysisResult:
507        """
508        Gap(P) = SAT(¬φ₁ ∧ ... ∧ ¬φₙ)
509
510        SAT  → coverage gap exists (DEFAULT-DENY reachable)
511        UNSAT → full coverage (every context matches a rule)
512        """
513        if not _Z3_AVAILABLE:
514            return _unavailable("coverage")
515        if not self.policy.rules:
516            return AnalysisResult(
517                kind="coverage", available=True, passed=False,
518                message="No rules — every context reaches DEFAULT-DENY",
519                gaps=[CoverageGap(description="Policy has no rules")],
520            )
521        tr, formulas = self._translate()
522        s = _z3.Solver()
523        s.add(*tr.domain_constraints())
524        s.add(*[_z3.Not(f) for f in formulas])
525        r = s.check()
526        if r == _z3.sat:
527            return AnalysisResult(
528                kind="coverage", available=True, passed=False,
529                message=f"Coverage gap found ({len(self.policy.rules)} rule(s) checked)",
530                gaps=[CoverageGap(
531                    description="Context region that falls through to DEFAULT-DENY",
532                    example_context=_model_dict(s.model(), tr),
533                )],
534            )
535        if r == _z3.unsat:
536            return AnalysisResult(
537                kind="coverage", available=True, passed=True,
538                message="Full coverage: every context matches at least one rule",
539            )
540        return AnalysisResult(
541            kind="coverage", available=True, passed=False,
542            message="Z3 returned unknown",
543        )
544
545    # ── shadows ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
546
547    @_z3_serialized
548    def check_shadows(self) -> AnalysisResult:
549        """
550        Reachable(rₖ) = SAT(¬φ₁ ∧ ... ∧ ¬φₖ₋₁ ∧ φₖ)
551
552        UNSAT → rule k is dead (shadowed by earlier rules).
553        """
554        if not _Z3_AVAILABLE:
555            return _unavailable("shadows")
556        if len(self.policy.rules) < 2:
557            return AnalysisResult(
558                kind="shadows", available=True, passed=True,
559                message="No shadowing possible with fewer than 2 rules",
560            )
561        tr, formulas = self._translate()
562        dead: list[ShadowedRule] = []
563        for k in range(1, len(formulas)):
564            s = _z3.Solver()
565            s.add(*tr.domain_constraints())
566            for i in range(k):
567                s.add(_z3.Not(formulas[i]))
568            s.add(formulas[k])
569            if s.check() == _z3.unsat:
570                rule = self.policy.rules[k]
571                dead.append(ShadowedRule(
572                    rule_index=k,
573                    condition=rule.condition,
574                    verdict=rule.verdict,
575                    shadowed_by=list(range(k)),
576                    description=(
577                        f"Rule [{k}] `when {rule.condition}` is unreachable; "
578                        f"rule(s) {list(range(k))} always match first"
579                    ),
580                ))
581        if dead:
582            return AnalysisResult(
583                kind="shadows", available=True, passed=False,
584                message=f"{len(dead)} dead rule(s) found",
585                shadows=dead,
586            )
587        return AnalysisResult(
588            kind="shadows", available=True, passed=True,
589            message="No dead rules detected",
590        )
591
592    # ── conflicts ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
593
594    @_z3_serialized
595    def check_conflicts(self) -> AnalysisResult:
596        """
597        Conflict(rᵢ, rⱼ) = SAT(φᵢ ∧ φⱼ) where vᵢ ≠ vⱼ
598
599        SAT → both rules can fire for the same context (first-match-wins
600        resolves it, but it may be unintentional).
601        """
602        if not _Z3_AVAILABLE:
603            return _unavailable("conflicts")
604        tr, formulas = self._translate()
605        rules = self.policy.rules
606        found: list[ConflictPair] = []
607        for i in range(len(formulas)):
608            for j in range(i + 1, len(formulas)):
609                if rules[i].verdict == rules[j].verdict:
610                    continue
611                s = _z3.Solver()
612                s.add(*tr.domain_constraints())
613                s.add(formulas[i], formulas[j])
614                if s.check() == _z3.sat:
615                    found.append(ConflictPair(
616                        rule_i=i,
617                        rule_j=j,
618                        verdict_i=rules[i].verdict,
619                        verdict_j=rules[j].verdict,
620                        example_context=_model_dict(s.model(), tr),
621                        description=(
622                            f"Rules [{i}] ({rules[i].verdict.value}) and "
623                            f"[{j}] ({rules[j].verdict.value}) overlap. "
