624                            f"First-match-wins resolves to rule [{i}]."
625                        ),
626                    ))
627        if found:
628            return AnalysisResult(
629                kind="conflicts", available=True, passed=False,
630                message=f"{len(found)} conflicting rule pair(s)",
631                conflicts=found,
632            )
633        return AnalysisResult(
634            kind="conflicts", available=True, passed=True,
635            message="No conflicts detected",
636        )
637
638    # ── equiv ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
639
640    @staticmethod
641    @_z3_serialized
642    def check_equiv(p1: TarlPolicy, p2: TarlPolicy) -> AnalysisResult:
643        """
644        SAT(V(P₁,c) ≠ V(P₂,c)) — not equivalent if satisfiable.
645        Uses a shared translator so both policies range over the same context.
646        """
647        if not _Z3_AVAILABLE:
648            return _unavailable("equiv")
649        tr = _ConditionToZ3()
650        tr.infer_types(p1.rules + p2.rules)
651        f1 = _build_formulas(tr, p1.rules)
652        f2 = _build_formulas(tr, p2.rules)
653        v1 = _verdict_ite(f1, p1.rules)
654        v2 = _verdict_ite(f2, p2.rules)
655        s = _z3.Solver()
656        s.add(*tr.domain_constraints())
657        s.add(v1 != v2)
658        r = s.check()
659        if r == _z3.unsat:
660            return AnalysisResult(
661                kind="equiv", available=True, passed=True,
662                message=f"'{p1.name}' and '{p2.name}' are equivalent",
663            )
664        if r == _z3.sat:
665            return AnalysisResult(
666                kind="equiv", available=True, passed=False,
667                message=f"'{p1.name}' and '{p2.name}' differ",
668                counterexample=_model_dict(s.model(), tr),
669            )
670        return AnalysisResult(
671            kind="equiv", available=True, passed=False,
672            message="Z3 returned unknown",
673        )
674
675    # ── refines ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
676
677    @staticmethod
678    @_z3_serialized
679    def check_refines(strict: TarlPolicy, permissive: TarlPolicy) -> AnalysisResult:
680        """
681        strict ⊑ permissive iff SAT(strict_allows ∧ ¬permissive_allows) is UNSAT.
682        I.e. strict never ALLOWs something that permissive would not ALLOW.
683        """
684        if not _Z3_AVAILABLE:
685            return _unavailable("refines")
686        tr = _ConditionToZ3()
687        tr.infer_types(strict.rules + permissive.rules)
688        fs = _build_formulas(tr, strict.rules)
689        fp = _build_formulas(tr, permissive.rules)
690        vs = _verdict_ite(fs, strict.rules)
691        vp = _verdict_ite(fp, permissive.rules)
692        _ALLOW = _z3.IntVal(_VERDICT_INT[TarlVerdict.ALLOW])
693        s = _z3.Solver()
694        s.add(*tr.domain_constraints())
695        s.add(vs == _ALLOW, vp != _ALLOW)
696        r = s.check()
697        if r == _z3.unsat:
698            return AnalysisResult(
699                kind="refines", available=True, passed=True,
700                message=(
701                    f"'{strict.name}' ⊑ '{permissive.name}': "
702                    "strict never allows what permissive would deny"
703                ),
704            )
705        if r == _z3.sat:
706            return AnalysisResult(
707                kind="refines", available=True, passed=False,
708                message=(
709                    f"'{strict.name}' is NOT a refinement of '{permissive.name}'"
710                ),
711                counterexample=_model_dict(s.model(), tr),
712            )
713        return AnalysisResult(
714            kind="refines", available=True, passed=False,
715            message="Z3 returned unknown",
716        )

Static analysis engine for a single T.A.R.L. policy.

All methods return AnalysisResult. When z3-solver is not installed every result has available=False and carries an installation hint.

PolicyAnalyzer(policy: TarlPolicy) 
494    def __init__(self, policy: TarlPolicy) -> None:
495        self.policy = policy
policy
def check_coverage(self) -> AnalysisResult: 
505    @_z3_serialized
506    def check_coverage(self) -> AnalysisResult:
507        """
508        Gap(P) = SAT(¬φ₁ ∧ ... ∧ ¬φₙ)
509
510        SAT  → coverage gap exists (DEFAULT-DENY reachable)
511        UNSAT → full coverage (every context matches a rule)
512        """
513        if not _Z3_AVAILABLE:
514            return _unavailable("coverage")
515        if not self.policy.rules:
516            return AnalysisResult(
517                kind="coverage", available=True, passed=False,
518                message="No rules — every context reaches DEFAULT-DENY",
519                gaps=[CoverageGap(description="Policy has no rules")],
520            )
521        tr, formulas = self._translate()
522        s = _z3.Solver()
523        s.add(*tr.domain_constraints())
524        s.add(*[_z3.Not(f) for f in formulas])
525        r = s.check()
526        if r == _z3.sat:
527            return AnalysisResult(
528                kind="coverage", available=True, passed=False,
529                message=f"Coverage gap found ({len(self.policy.rules)} rule(s) checked)",
530                gaps=[CoverageGap(
531                    description="Context region that falls through to DEFAULT-DENY",
532                    example_context=_model_dict(s.model(), tr),
533                )],
534            )
535        if r == _z3.unsat:
536            return AnalysisResult(
537                kind="coverage", available=True, passed=True,
538                message="Full coverage: every context matches at least one rule",
539            )
540        return AnalysisResult(
541            kind="coverage", available=True, passed=False,
542            message="Z3 returned unknown",
543        )

Gap(P) = SAT(¬φ₁ ∧ ... ∧ ¬φₙ)

SAT → coverage gap exists (DEFAULT-DENY reachable) UNSAT → full coverage (every context matches a rule)

def check_shadows(self) -> AnalysisResult: 
547    @_z3_serialized
548    def check_shadows(self) -> AnalysisResult:
549        """
550        Reachable(rₖ) = SAT(¬φ₁ ∧ ... ∧ ¬φₖ₋₁ ∧ φₖ)
551
552        UNSAT → rule k is dead (shadowed by earlier rules).
553        """
554        if not _Z3_AVAILABLE:
555            return _unavailable("shadows")
556        if len(self.policy.rules) < 2:
557            return AnalysisResult(
558                kind="shadows", available=True, passed=True,
559                message="No shadowing possible with fewer than 2 rules",
560            )
561        tr, formulas = self._translate()
562        dead: list[ShadowedRule] = []
563        for k in range(1, len(formulas)):
564            s = _z3.Solver()
565            s.add(*tr.domain_constraints())
566            for i in range(k):
567                s.add(_z3.Not(formulas[i]))
568            s.add(formulas[k])
569            if s.check() == _z3.unsat:
570                rule = self.policy.rules[k]
571                dead.append(ShadowedRule(
572                    rule_index=k,
573                    condition=rule.condition,
574                    verdict=rule.verdict,
575                    shadowed_by=list(range(k)),
576                    description=(
577                        f"Rule [{k}] `when {rule.condition}` is unreachable; "
578                        f"rule(s) {list(range(k))} always match first"
579                    ),
580                ))
581        if dead:
582            return AnalysisResult(
583                kind="shadows", available=True, passed=False,
584                message=f"{len(dead)} dead rule(s) found",
585                shadows=dead,
586            )
587        return AnalysisResult(
588            kind="shadows", available=True, passed=True,
589            message="No dead rules detected",
590        )

Reachable(rₖ) = SAT(¬φ₁ ∧ ... ∧ ¬φₖ₋₁ ∧ φₖ)

UNSAT → rule k is dead (shadowed by earlier rules).

def check_conflicts(self) -> AnalysisResult: 
594    @_z3_serialized
595    def check_conflicts(self) -> AnalysisResult:
596        """
597        Conflict(rᵢ, rⱼ) = SAT(φᵢ ∧ φⱼ) where vᵢ ≠ vⱼ
598
599        SAT → both rules can fire for the same context (first-match-wins
600        resolves it, but it may be unintentional).
601        """
602        if not _Z3_AVAILABLE:
603            return _unavailable("conflicts")
604        tr, formulas = self._translate()
605        rules = self.policy.rules
606        found: list[ConflictPair] = []
607        for i in range(len(formulas)):
608            for j in range(i + 1, len(formulas)):
609                if rules[i].verdict == rules[j].verdict:
610                    continue
611                s = _z3.Solver()
612                s.add(*tr.domain_constraints())
613                s.add(formulas[i], formulas[j])
614                if s.check() == _z3.sat:
615                    found.append(ConflictPair(
616                        rule_i=i,
617                        rule_j=j,
618                        verdict_i=rules[i].verdict,
619                        verdict_j=rules[j].verdict,
620                        example_context=_model_dict(s.model(), tr),
621                        description=(
622                            f"Rules [{i}] ({rules[i].verdict.value}) and "
623                            f"[{j}] ({rules[j].verdict.value}) overlap. "
624                            f"First-match-wins resolves to rule [{i}]."
625                        ),
626                    ))
627        if found:
628            return AnalysisResult(
629                kind="conflicts", available=True, passed=False,
630                message=f"{len(found)} conflicting rule pair(s)",
631                conflicts=found,
632            )
633        return AnalysisResult(
634            kind="conflicts", available=True, passed=True,
635            message="No conflicts detected",
636        )

Conflict(rᵢ, rⱼ) = SAT(φᵢ ∧ φⱼ) where vᵢ ≠ vⱼ

SAT → both rules can fire for the same context (first-match-wins resolves it, but it may be unintentional).

@staticmethod
def check_equiv( p1: TarlPolicy, p2: TarlPolicy) -> AnalysisResult: 
640    @staticmethod
641    @_z3_serialized
642    def check_equiv(p1: TarlPolicy, p2: TarlPolicy) -> AnalysisResult:
643        """
644        SAT(V(P₁,c) ≠ V(P₂,c)) — not equivalent if satisfiable.
645        Uses a shared translator so both policies range over the same context.
646        """
647        if not _Z3_AVAILABLE:
648            return _unavailable("equiv")
649        tr = _ConditionToZ3()
650        tr.infer_types(p1.rules + p2.rules)
651        f1 = _build_formulas(tr, p1.rules)
652        f2 = _build_formulas(tr, p2.rules)
653        v1 = _verdict_ite(f1, p1.rules)
654        v2 = _verdict_ite(f2, p2.rules)
655        s = _z3.Solver()
656        s.add(*tr.domain_constraints())
657        s.add(v1 != v2)
658        r = s.check()
659        if r == _z3.unsat:
660            return AnalysisResult(
661                kind="equiv", available=True, passed=True,
662                message=f"'{p1.name}' and '{p2.name}' are equivalent",
663            )
664        if r == _z3.sat:
665            return AnalysisResult(
666                kind="equiv", available=True, passed=False,
667                message=f"'{p1.name}' and '{p2.name}' differ",
668                counterexample=_model_dict(s.model(), tr),
669            )
670        return AnalysisResult(
671            kind="equiv", available=True, passed=False,
672            message="Z3 returned unknown",
673        )

SAT(V(P₁,c) ≠ V(P₂,c)) — not equivalent if satisfiable. Uses a shared translator so both policies range over the same context.

@staticmethod
def check_refines( strict: TarlPolicy, permissive: TarlPolicy) -> AnalysisResult: 
677    @staticmethod
678    @_z3_serialized
679    def check_refines(strict: TarlPolicy, permissive: TarlPolicy) -> AnalysisResult:
680        """
681        strict ⊑ permissive iff SAT(strict_allows ∧ ¬permissive_allows) is UNSAT.
682        I.e. strict never ALLOWs something that permissive would not ALLOW.
683        """
684        if not _Z3_AVAILABLE:
685            return _unavailable("refines")
686        tr = _ConditionToZ3()
687        tr.infer_types(strict.rules + permissive.rules)
688        fs = _build_formulas(tr, strict.rules)
689        fp = _build_formulas(tr, permissive.rules)
690        vs = _verdict_ite(fs, strict.rules)
691        vp = _verdict_ite(fp, permissive.rules)
692        _ALLOW = _z3.IntVal(_VERDICT_INT[TarlVerdict.ALLOW])
693        s = _z3.Solver()
694        s.add(*tr.domain_constraints())
695        s.add(vs == _ALLOW, vp != _ALLOW)
696        r = s.check()
697        if r == _z3.unsat:
698            return AnalysisResult(
699                kind="refines", available=True, passed=True,
700                message=(
701                    f"'{strict.name}' ⊑ '{permissive.name}': "
702                    "strict never allows what permissive would deny"
703                ),
704            )
705        if r == _z3.sat:
706            return AnalysisResult(
707                kind="refines", available=True, passed=False,
708                message=(
709                    f"'{strict.name}' is NOT a refinement of '{permissive.name}'"
710                ),
711                counterexample=_model_dict(s.model(), tr),
712            )
713        return AnalysisResult(
714            kind="refines", available=True, passed=False,
715            message="Z3 returned unknown",
716        )

strict ⊑ permissive iff SAT(strict_allows ∧ ¬permissive_allows) is UNSAT. I.e. strict never ALLOWs something that permissive would not ALLOW.

@dataclass
class AnalysisResult: 
121@dataclass
122class AnalysisResult:
123    """Result of a static analysis query."""
124    kind: str
125    available: bool
126    passed: bool
127    message: str = ""
128    gaps: list[CoverageGap] = field(default_factory=list)
129    shadows: list[ShadowedRule] = field(default_factory=list)
130    conflicts: list[ConflictPair] = field(default_factory=list)
131    counterexample: dict[str, Any] | None = None
132
133    def __str__(self) -> str:
134        if not self.available:
135            return f"[{self.kind}] unavailable: {self.message}"
136        status = "PASS" if self.passed else "FAIL"
137        return f"[{self.kind}] {status}: {self.message}"
138
139    @property
140    def summary(self) -> str:
141        lines = [str(self)]
142        for g in self.gaps:
143            lines.append(f"  gap: {g.description}")
144            if g.example_context:
145                lines.append(f"    example: {g.example_context}")
146        for s in self.shadows:
147            lines.append(f"  dead: {s.description}")
148        for c in self.conflicts:
149            lines.append(f"  conflict: {c.description}")
150            if c.example_context:
151                lines.append(f"    example: {c.example_context}")
152        if self.counterexample:
153            lines.append(f"  counterexample: {self.counterexample}")
154        return "\n".join(lines)

Result of a static analysis query.

AnalysisResult( kind: str, available: bool, passed: bool, message: str = '', gaps: list[CoverageGap] = <factory>, shadows: list[ShadowedRule] = <factory>, conflicts: list[ConflictPair] = <factory>, counterexample: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None)
kind: str
available: bool
passed: bool
message: str = ''
gaps: list[CoverageGap]
shadows: list[ShadowedRule]
conflicts: list[ConflictPair]
counterexample: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None
summary: str 
139    @property
140    def summary(self) -> str:
141        lines = [str(self)]
142        for g in self.gaps:
143            lines.append(f"  gap: {g.description}")
144            if g.example_context:
145                lines.append(f"    example: {g.example_context}")
146        for s in self.shadows:
147            lines.append(f"  dead: {s.description}")
148        for c in self.conflicts:
149            lines.append(f"  conflict: {c.description}")
150            if c.example_context:
151                lines.append(f"    example: {c.example_context}")
152        if self.counterexample:
153            lines.append(f"  counterexample: {self.counterexample}")
154        return "\n".join(lines)
@dataclass
class CoverageGap: 
93@dataclass
94class CoverageGap:
95    """A context region where all rules fail and DEFAULT-DENY applies."""
96    description: str = ""
97    example_context: dict[str, Any] | None = None

A context region where all rules fail and DEFAULT-DENY applies.

CoverageGap( description: str = '', example_context: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None)
description: str = ''
example_context: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None
@dataclass
class ShadowedRule: 
100@dataclass
101class ShadowedRule:
102    """A rule that can never be reached because earlier rules always fire first."""
103    rule_index: int = 0
104    condition: str = ""
105    verdict: TarlVerdict = TarlVerdict.DENY
106    shadowed_by: list[int] = field(default_factory=list)
107    description: str = ""

A rule that can never be reached because earlier rules always fire first.

ShadowedRule( rule_index: int = 0, condition: str = '', verdict: TarlVerdict = <TarlVerdict.DENY: 'DENY'>, shadowed_by: list[int] = <factory>, description: str = '')
rule_index: int = 0
condition: str = ''
verdict: TarlVerdict = <TarlVerdict.DENY: 'DENY'>
shadowed_by: list[int]
description: str = ''
@dataclass
class ConflictPair: 
110@dataclass
111class ConflictPair:
112    """Two rules with different verdicts that can simultaneously match."""
113    rule_i: int = 0
114    rule_j: int = 0
115    verdict_i: TarlVerdict = TarlVerdict.DENY
116    verdict_j: TarlVerdict = TarlVerdict.DENY
117    example_context: dict[str, Any] | None = None
118    description: str = ""

Two rules with different verdicts that can simultaneously match.

ConflictPair( rule_i: int = 0, rule_j: int = 0, verdict_i: TarlVerdict = <TarlVerdict.DENY: 'DENY'>, verdict_j: TarlVerdict = <TarlVerdict.DENY: 'DENY'>, example_context: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None, description: str = '')
rule_i: int = 0
rule_j: int = 0
verdict_i: TarlVerdict = <TarlVerdict.DENY: 'DENY'>
verdict_j: TarlVerdict = <TarlVerdict.DENY: 'DENY'>
example_context: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None
description: str = ''
class ProofVerifier: 
105class ProofVerifier:
106    """
107    Verifies TarlProof certificates independently of the runtime.
108
109    Usage::
110
111        verifier = ProofVerifier()
112        verifier.add_hmac_key("key1", b"my-secret")
113        verifier.add_ed25519_key("key2", ed25519_public_key)
114        result = verifier.verify(proof, policy_source=policy_text)
115        assert result.valid
116    """
117
118    def __init__(
119        self,
120        require_signature: bool = False,
121        allowed_signature_algorithms: set[str] | None = None,
122        require_policy_source: bool = False,
123        max_age_seconds: float | None = None,
124        revoked_policy_hashes: set[str] | None = None,
125        replay_guard: ReplayGuard | None = None,
126    ) -> None:
127        """
128        Strict-mode options (all default to today's permissive behavior):
129
130          require_signature             — an unsigned proof is INVALID.
131          allowed_signature_algorithms  — restrict accepted signature families
132                                          (e.g. {"ed25519"} rejects HMAC proofs).
133                                          Compared against the algorithm family
134                                          (``hmac`` / ``ed25519``).
135          require_policy_source         — verification without a policy_source to
136                                          bind policy_hash against is INVALID.
137          max_age_seconds               — reject proofs whose evaluated_at is
138                                          older than this (freshness; C024).
139          revoked_policy_hashes         — proofs bound to a revoked policy hash
140                                          are INVALID (policy revocation; C024).
141          replay_guard                  — a ReplayGuard; a proof already seen is
142                                          rejected as a replay (C023/C024).
143        """
144        self._hmac_keys: dict[str, bytes] = {}
145        self._ed25519_keys: dict[str, Ed25519PublicKey] = {}
146        self.require_signature = require_signature
147        self.allowed_signature_algorithms = (
148            {a.lower() for a in allowed_signature_algorithms}
149            if allowed_signature_algorithms is not None
150            else None
151        )
152        self.require_policy_source = require_policy_source
153        self.max_age_seconds = max_age_seconds
154        self.revoked_policy_hashes = revoked_policy_hashes or set()
155        self.replay_guard = replay_guard
156
157    def add_hmac_key(self, key_id: str, secret: bytes) -> ProofVerifier:
158        """Register an HMAC-SHA256 key for signature verification."""
159        self._hmac_keys[key_id] = secret
160        return self
161
162    def add_ed25519_key(
163        self, key_id: str, public_key: bytes | Ed25519PublicKey
164    ) -> ProofVerifier:
165        """Register an Ed25519 public key for signature verification."""
166        if isinstance(public_key, Ed25519PublicKey):
167            key = public_key
168        else:
169            key = Ed25519PublicKey.from_public_bytes(public_key)
170        self._ed25519_keys[key_id] = key
171        return self
172
173    def verify(
174        self,
175        proof: TarlProof,
176        policy_source: str | None = None,
177        expected_context: dict | None = None,
178        now: datetime.datetime | None = None,
179    ) -> VerificationResult:
180        """
181        Verify a TarlProof.
182
183        Checks performed:
184          signature    — HMAC-SHA256 or Ed25519 valid (or None if unsigned)
185          policy_hash  — matches provided policy_source (or None if not given)
186          trace        — internal consistency (k is first True in T, verdict correct)
187
188        valid = True requires:
189          - signature is True or None (unsigned)
190          - policy_hash is True or None (not provided)
191          - trace is True
192        """
193        checks: dict[str, bool | None] = {}
194        messages: list[str] = []
195
196        # ── 1. Signature ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
197        sig_result = self._check_signature(proof)
198        if self.require_signature and sig_result is None:
199            # Strict mode: an unsigned proof is not acceptable.
200            sig_result = False
201        checks["signature"] = sig_result
202        if sig_result is True:
203            messages.append("signature valid")
204        elif sig_result is False:
205            messages.append(
206                "signature INVALID"
207                if proof.signature or not self.require_signature
208                else "signature REQUIRED but proof is unsigned"
209            )
210        else:
211            messages.append("signature skipped (unsigned)")
212
213        # ── 2. Policy hash ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
214        if policy_source is not None:
215            ph_ok = _check_policy_hash(proof, policy_source)
216            checks["policy_hash"] = ph_ok
217            messages.append(
218                "policy hash valid" if ph_ok else "policy hash MISMATCH"
219            )
220        elif self.require_policy_source:
221            # Strict mode: cannot certify policy binding without the source.
222            checks["policy_hash"] = False
223            messages.append("policy source REQUIRED but not provided")
224        else:
225            checks["policy_hash"] = None
226
227        # ── 3. Trace consistency ──────────────────────────────────────────────
228        trace_ok = _check_trace(proof)
229        checks["trace"] = trace_ok
230        messages.append(
231            "trace consistent" if trace_ok else "trace INCONSISTENT"
232        )
233
234        # ── 4. Context binding (C023: replay an old proof for a new context) ──
235        if expected_context is not None:
236            cb_ok = proof.context_hash == canonical_context_hash(expected_context)
237            checks["context_binding"] = cb_ok
238            messages.append(
239                "context binds" if cb_ok else "context hash MISMATCH"
240            )
241        else:
242            checks["context_binding"] = None
243
244        # ── 5. Freshness (C024: replay a stale ALLOW) ─────────────────────────
245        if self.max_age_seconds is not None:
246            fr_ok = _check_freshness(proof, self.max_age_seconds, now)
247            checks["freshness"] = fr_ok
248            messages.append("fresh" if fr_ok else "proof is STALE")
249        else:
250            checks["freshness"] = None
251
252        # ── 6. Policy revocation (C024) ───────────────────────────────────────
253        if self.revoked_policy_hashes:
254            nr_ok = proof.policy_hash not in self.revoked_policy_hashes
255            checks["not_revoked"] = nr_ok
256            messages.append("policy current" if nr_ok else "policy REVOKED")
257        else:
258            checks["not_revoked"] = None
259
260        # ── 7. Replay (exact reuse of a previously-accepted proof) ────────────
261        if self.replay_guard is not None:
262            fresh = self.replay_guard.check_and_record(proof)
263            checks["not_replayed"] = fresh
264            messages.append("first use" if fresh else "REPLAYED proof")
265        else:
266            checks["not_replayed"] = None
267
268        valid = (
269            sig_result is not False
270            and checks["policy_hash"] is not False
271            and trace_ok
272            and checks["context_binding"] is not False
273            and checks["freshness"] is not False
274            and checks["not_revoked"] is not False
275            and checks["not_replayed"] is not False
276        )
277
278        return VerificationResult(
279            valid=valid,
280            checks=checks,
281            message="; ".join(messages),
282        )
283
284    def _check_signature(self, proof: TarlProof) -> bool | None:
285        """
286        Returns True if the signature is cryptographically valid.
287        Returns False if invalid or the key is unknown.
288        Returns None if the proof has no signature.
289        """
290        if not proof.signature:
291            return None
292
293        alg, sep, sig_hex = proof.signature.partition(":")
294        if not sep:
295            return False
296
297        # Strict mode: reject signature families outside the allow-list (e.g.
298        # an HMAC proof presented to an Ed25519-only verifier).
299        if self.allowed_signature_algorithms is not None:
300            family = alg.split("-", 1)[0].lower()
301            if family not in self.allowed_signature_algorithms:
302                return False
303
304        if alg == "hmac-sha256":
305            secret = self._hmac_keys.get(proof.key_id)
306            if secret is None:
307                return False
308            expected = hmac.new(
309                secret, proof.canonical_bytes(), hashlib.sha256
310            ).hexdigest()
311            return hmac.compare_digest(expected, sig_hex)
312
313        if alg == "ed25519":
314            public_key = self._ed25519_keys.get(proof.key_id)
315            if public_key is None:
316                return False
317            try:
318                public_key.verify(
319                    bytes.fromhex(sig_hex), proof.canonical_bytes()
320                )
321                return True
322            except (ValueError, InvalidSignature):
323                return False
324
325        return False  # unknown algorithm

Verifies TarlProof certificates independently of the runtime.

Usage::

verifier = ProofVerifier()
verifier.add_hmac_key("key1", b"my-secret")
verifier.add_ed25519_key("key2", ed25519_public_key)
result = verifier.verify(proof, policy_source=policy_text)
assert result.valid
ProofVerifier( require_signature: bool = False, allowed_signature_algorithms: set[str] | None = None, require_policy_source: bool = False, max_age_seconds: float | None = None, revoked_policy_hashes: set[str] | None = None, replay_guard: utf.tarl.verifier.ReplayGuard | None = None) 
118    def __init__(
119        self,
120        require_signature: bool = False,
121        allowed_signature_algorithms: set[str] | None = None,
122        require_policy_source: bool = False,
123        max_age_seconds: float | None = None,
124        revoked_policy_hashes: set[str] | None = None,
125        replay_guard: ReplayGuard | None = None,
126    ) -> None:
127        """
128        Strict-mode options (all default to today's permissive behavior):
129
130          require_signature             — an unsigned proof is INVALID.
131          allowed_signature_algorithms  — restrict accepted signature families
132                                          (e.g. {"ed25519"} rejects HMAC proofs).
133                                          Compared against the algorithm family
134                                          (``hmac`` / ``ed25519``).
135          require_policy_source         — verification without a policy_source to
136                                          bind policy_hash against is INVALID.
137          max_age_seconds               — reject proofs whose evaluated_at is
138                                          older than this (freshness; C024).
139          revoked_policy_hashes         — proofs bound to a revoked policy hash
140                                          are INVALID (policy revocation; C024).
141          replay_guard                  — a ReplayGuard; a proof already seen is
142                                          rejected as a replay (C023/C024).
143        """
144        self._hmac_keys: dict[str, bytes] = {}
145        self._ed25519_keys: dict[str, Ed25519PublicKey] = {}
146        self.require_signature = require_signature
147        self.allowed_signature_algorithms = (
148            {a.lower() for a in allowed_signature_algorithms}
149            if allowed_signature_algorithms is not None
150            else None
151        )
152        self.require_policy_source = require_policy_source
153        self.max_age_seconds = max_age_seconds
154        self.revoked_policy_hashes = revoked_policy_hashes or set()
155        self.replay_guard = replay_guard

Strict-mode options (all default to today's permissive behavior):

require_signature — an unsigned proof is INVALID. allowed_signature_algorithms — restrict accepted signature families (e.g. {"ed25519"} rejects HMAC proofs). Compared against the algorithm family (hmac / ed25519). require_policy_source — verification without a policy_source to bind policy_hash against is INVALID. max_age_seconds — reject proofs whose evaluated_at is older than this (freshness; C024). revoked_policy_hashes — proofs bound to a revoked policy hash are INVALID (policy revocation; C024). replay_guard — a ReplayGuard; a proof already seen is rejected as a replay (C023/C024).

require_signature
allowed_signature_algorithms
require_policy_source
max_age_seconds
revoked_policy_hashes
replay_guard
def add_hmac_key(self, key_id: str, secret: bytes) -> ProofVerifier: 
157    def add_hmac_key(self, key_id: str, secret: bytes) -> ProofVerifier:
158        """Register an HMAC-SHA256 key for signature verification."""
159        self._hmac_keys[key_id] = secret
160        return self

Register an HMAC-SHA256 key for signature verification.

def add_ed25519_key( self, key_id: str, public_key: bytes | cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.ed25519.Ed25519PublicKey) -> ProofVerifier: 
162    def add_ed25519_key(
163        self, key_id: str, public_key: bytes | Ed25519PublicKey
164    ) -> ProofVerifier:
165        """Register an Ed25519 public key for signature verification."""
166        if isinstance(public_key, Ed25519PublicKey):
167            key = public_key
168        else:
169            key = Ed25519PublicKey.from_public_bytes(public_key)
170        self._ed25519_keys[key_id] = key
171        return self

Register an Ed25519 public key for signature verification.

def verify( self, proof: TarlProof, policy_source: str | None = None, expected_context: dict | None = None, now: datetime.datetime | None = None) -> VerificationResult: 
173    def verify(
174        self,
175        proof: TarlProof,
176        policy_source: str | None = None,
177        expected_context: dict | None = None,
178        now: datetime.datetime | None = None,
179    ) -> VerificationResult:
180        """
181        Verify a TarlProof.
182
183        Checks performed:
184          signature    — HMAC-SHA256 or Ed25519 valid (or None if unsigned)
185          policy_hash  — matches provided policy_source (or None if not given)
186          trace        — internal consistency (k is first True in T, verdict correct)
187
188        valid = True requires:
189          - signature is True or None (unsigned)
190          - policy_hash is True or None (not provided)
191          - trace is True
192        """
193        checks: dict[str, bool | None] = {}
194        messages: list[str] = []
195
196        # ── 1. Signature ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
197        sig_result = self._check_signature(proof)
198        if self.require_signature and sig_result is None:
199            # Strict mode: an unsigned proof is not acceptable.
200            sig_result = False
201        checks["signature"] = sig_result
202        if sig_result is True:
203            messages.append("signature valid")
204        elif sig_result is False:
205            messages.append(
206                "signature INVALID"
207                if proof.signature or not self.require_signature
208                else "signature REQUIRED but proof is unsigned"
209            )
210        else:
211            messages.append("signature skipped (unsigned)")
212
213        # ── 2. Policy hash ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
214        if policy_source is not None:
215            ph_ok = _check_policy_hash(proof, policy_source)
216            checks["policy_hash"] = ph_ok
217            messages.append(
218                "policy hash valid" if ph_ok else "policy hash MISMATCH"
219            )
220        elif self.require_policy_source:
221            # Strict mode: cannot certify policy binding without the source.
222            checks["policy_hash"] = False
223            messages.append("policy source REQUIRED but not provided")
224        else:
225            checks["policy_hash"] = None
226
227        # ── 3. Trace consistency ──────────────────────────────────────────────
228        trace_ok = _check_trace(proof)
229        checks["trace"] = trace_ok
230        messages.append(
231            "trace consistent" if trace_ok else "trace INCONSISTENT"
232        )
233
234        # ── 4. Context binding (C023: replay an old proof for a new context) ──
235        if expected_context is not None:
236            cb_ok = proof.context_hash == canonical_context_hash(expected_context)
237            checks["context_binding"] = cb_ok
238            messages.append(
239                "context binds" if cb_ok else "context hash MISMATCH"
240            )
241        else:
242            checks["context_binding"] = None
243
244        # ── 5. Freshness (C024: replay a stale ALLOW) ─────────────────────────
245        if self.max_age_seconds is not None:
246            fr_ok = _check_freshness(proof, self.max_age_seconds, now)
247            checks["freshness"] = fr_ok
248            messages.append("fresh" if fr_ok else "proof is STALE")
249        else:
250            checks["freshness"] = None
251
252        # ── 6. Policy revocation (C024) ───────────────────────────────────────
253        if self.revoked_policy_hashes:
254            nr_ok = proof.policy_hash not in self.revoked_policy_hashes
255            checks["not_revoked"] = nr_ok
256            messages.append("policy current" if nr_ok else "policy REVOKED")
257        else:
258            checks["not_revoked"] = None
259
260        # ── 7. Replay (exact reuse of a previously-accepted proof) ────────────
261        if self.replay_guard is not None:
262            fresh = self.replay_guard.check_and_record(proof)
263            checks["not_replayed"] = fresh
264            messages.append("first use" if fresh else "REPLAYED proof")
265        else:
266            checks["not_replayed"] = None
267
268        valid = (
269            sig_result is not False
270            and checks["policy_hash"] is not False
271            and trace_ok
272            and checks["context_binding"] is not False
273            and checks["freshness"] is not False
274            and checks["not_revoked"] is not False
275            and checks["not_replayed"] is not False
276        )
277
278        return VerificationResult(
279            valid=valid,
280            checks=checks,
281            message="; ".join(messages),
282        )

Verify a TarlProof.

Checks performed: signature — HMAC-SHA256 or Ed25519 valid (or None if unsigned) policy_hash — matches provided policy_source (or None if not given) trace — internal consistency (k is first True in T, verdict correct)

valid = True requires:

  • signature is True or None (unsigned)
  • policy_hash is True or None (not provided)
  • trace is True
@dataclass
class VerificationResult: 
 80@dataclass
 81class VerificationResult:
 82    """Result of verifying a TarlProof."""
 83    valid: bool
 84    checks: dict[str, bool | None] = field(default_factory=dict)
 85    message: str = ""
 86
 87    def __str__(self) -> str:
 88        status = "VALID" if self.valid else "INVALID"
 89        return f"[{status}] {self.message}"
 90
 91    @property
 92    def summary(self) -> str:
 93        lines = [str(self)]
 94        for check, result in self.checks.items():
 95            if result is True:
 96                mark = "✓"
 97            elif result is False:
 98                mark = "✗"
 99            else:
100                mark = "-"
101            lines.append(f"  {mark} {check}")
102        return "\n".join(lines)

Result of verifying a TarlProof.

VerificationResult( valid: bool, checks: dict[str, bool | None] = <factory>, message: str = '')
valid: bool
checks: dict[str, bool | None]
message: str = ''
summary: str 
 91    @property
 92    def summary(self) -> str:
 93        lines = [str(self)]
 94        for check, result in self.checks.items():
 95            if result is True:
 96                mark = "✓"
 97            elif result is False:
 98                mark = "✗"
 99            else:
100                mark = "-"
101            lines.append(f"  {mark} {check}")
102        return "\n".join(lines)
class TarlAuditArchive: 
 52class TarlAuditArchive:
 53    """
 54    Append-only audit log of TarlProof certificates backed by SQLite.
 55
 56    Thread-safe: concurrent stores and queries are serialised by an
 57    internal lock.  Use the context-manager form to ensure the connection
 58    is closed properly::
 59
 60        with TarlAuditArchive("tarl_audit.db") as archive:
 61            archive.store(proof)
 62
 63    Or manage the lifecycle manually with :meth:`close`.
 64    """
 65
 66    def __init__(self, db_path: str = "tarl_audit.db") -> None:
 67        self._path = db_path
 68        self._conn: sqlite3.Connection | None = None
 69        self._lock = threading.Lock()
 70        self._ensure_schema()
 71
 72    # ── Schema ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 73
 74    def _connect(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
 75        if self._conn is None:
 76            self._conn = sqlite3.connect(
 77                self._path, check_same_thread=False
 78            )
 79        return self._conn
 80
 81    def _ensure_schema(self) -> None:
 82        with self._lock:
 83            conn = self._connect()
 84            conn.executescript("""
 85                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS proofs (
 86                    id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
 87                    policy_hash   TEXT    NOT NULL,
 88                    context_hash  TEXT    NOT NULL,
 89                    verdict       TEXT    NOT NULL,
 90                    rule_index    INTEGER NOT NULL,
 91                    evaluated_at  TEXT    NOT NULL,
 92                    expires_at    TEXT,
 93                    signature     TEXT,
 94                    proof_json    TEXT    NOT NULL,
 95                    prev_hash     TEXT,
 96                    entry_hash    TEXT
 97                );
 98                CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_verdict
 99                    ON proofs (verdict);
100                CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_evaluated_at
101                    ON proofs (evaluated_at);
102            """)
103            # Migrate older archives that predate the hash chain.
104            existing = {r[1] for r in conn.execute(
105                "PRAGMA table_info(proofs)").fetchall()}
106            for col in ("prev_hash", "entry_hash"):
107                if col not in existing:
108                    conn.execute(f"ALTER TABLE proofs ADD COLUMN {col} TEXT")
109            conn.commit()
110
111    # ── Write ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
112
113    def store(
114        self,
115        proof: TarlProof,
116        expires_at: str | None = None,
117    ) -> int:
118        """
119        Persist a proof in the archive.
120
121        :param proof:       The TarlProof to store.
122        :param expires_at:  Optional ISO-8601 UTC expiry from TarlDecision.expires_at.
123        :returns:           Row id of the inserted record.
124        """
125        proof_json = proof.to_json()
126        with self._lock:
127            conn = self._connect()
128            # Link this record to the current chain head (genesis if empty).
129            head = conn.execute(
130                "SELECT entry_hash FROM proofs ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1"
131            ).fetchone()
132            prev_hash = head[0] if head and head[0] else _GENESIS
133            entry_hash = _entry_hash(prev_hash, proof_json)
134            cursor = conn.execute(
135                """INSERT INTO proofs
136                   (policy_hash, context_hash, verdict, rule_index,
137                    evaluated_at, expires_at, signature, proof_json,
138                    prev_hash, entry_hash)
139                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
140                (
141                    proof.policy_hash,
142                    proof.context_hash,
143                    proof.verdict.value,
144                    proof.rule_index,
145                    proof.evaluated_at,
146                    expires_at,
147                    proof.signature or None,
148                    proof_json,
149                    prev_hash,
150                    entry_hash,
151                ),
152            )
153            conn.commit()
154            # lastrowid is the new AUTOINCREMENT id after a successful INSERT;
155            # it is only None if no row was inserted (never on this path).
156            return cursor.lastrowid or 0
157
158    # ── Read ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
159
160    def query(
161        self,
162        verdict: str | None = None,
163        from_dt: str | None = None,
164        to_dt: str | None = None,
165        limit: int = 100,
166        verifier=None,
167    ) -> list[TarlProof]:
168        """
169        Query stored proofs, newest first.
170
171        :param verdict:   Filter by verdict ("ALLOW", "DENY", or "ESCALATE").
172        :param from_dt:   ISO-8601 lower bound on evaluated_at (inclusive).
173        :param to_dt:     ISO-8601 upper bound on evaluated_at (inclusive).
174        :param limit:     Maximum number of results (default 100).
175        :param verifier:  Optional ProofVerifier.  When supplied, proofs whose
176                          signature check returns False (cryptographically
177                          invalid) are excluded from results.  Unsigned proofs
178                          (signature="") are kept; only actively invalid
179                          signatures are rejected.  Without a verifier, rows
180                          are returned as-is from SQLite with no tamper check
181                          — callers that need tamper-evidence must pass one.
182        :returns:         List of TarlProof objects.
183        """
184        conditions: list[str] = []
185        params: list = []
186        if verdict:
187            conditions.append("verdict = ?")
188            params.append(verdict.upper())
189        if from_dt:
190            conditions.append("evaluated_at >= ?")
191            params.append(from_dt)
192        if to_dt:
193            conditions.append("evaluated_at <= ?")
194            params.append(to_dt)
195        where = ("WHERE " + " AND ".join(conditions)) if conditions else ""
196        params.append(limit)
197        with self._lock:
198            conn = self._connect()
199            rows = conn.execute(
200                f"SELECT proof_json FROM proofs {where} "
201                f"ORDER BY evaluated_at DESC LIMIT ?",
202                params,
203            ).fetchall()
204        proofs = [TarlProof.from_json(row[0]) for row in rows]
205        if verifier is not None:
206            proofs = [
207                p for p in proofs
208                if verifier.verify(p).checks.get("signature") is not False
209            ]
210        return proofs
211
212    def count(
213        self,
214        verdict: str | None = None,
215        from_dt: str | None = None,
216        to_dt: str | None = None,
217    ) -> int:
218        """Count proofs matching the given filters."""
219        conditions: list[str] = []
220        params: list = []
221        if verdict:
222            conditions.append("verdict = ?")
223            params.append(verdict.upper())
224        if from_dt:
225            conditions.append("evaluated_at >= ?")
226            params.append(from_dt)
227        if to_dt:
228            conditions.append("evaluated_at <= ?")
229            params.append(to_dt)
230        where = ("WHERE " + " AND ".join(conditions)) if conditions else ""
231        with self._lock:
232            conn = self._connect()
233            row = conn.execute(
234                f"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM proofs {where}", params
235            ).fetchone()
236        return int(row[0]) if row else 0
237
238    # ── Tamper-evidence ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
239
240    def head_hash(self) -> str:
241        """Return the current chain head (latest entry_hash), or genesis if empty.
242
243        Persist this externally as a checkpoint: a later ``verify_chain`` whose
244        head differs from a trusted checkpoint reveals suffix rewriting even if
245        the chain re-links internally."""
246        with self._lock:
247            conn = self._connect()
248            row = conn.execute(
249                "SELECT entry_hash FROM proofs ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1"
250            ).fetchone()
251        return str(row[0]) if row and row[0] else _GENESIS
252
253    def verify_chain(
254        self, expected_head: str | None = None
255    ) -> ChainVerification:
256        """Walk the hash chain in insertion order and report its integrity.
257
258        Detects record tampering (a modified ``proof_json`` no longer hashes to
259        its stored ``entry_hash``), deletion/reordering (a record's ``prev_hash``
260        no longer matches its predecessor's ``entry_hash``), and a tampered head.
261
262        ``expected_head`` is a trusted external checkpoint (see :meth:`head_hash`
263        and ``tarl audit checkpoint``). A chain that re-links internally but
264        whose head differs from the checkpoint reveals suffix rewriting or
265        truncation — the case the internal walk alone cannot catch.
266        """
267        with self._lock:
268            conn = self._connect()
269            rows = conn.execute(
270                "SELECT id, proof_json, prev_hash, entry_hash "
271                "FROM proofs ORDER BY id ASC"
272            ).fetchall()
273        prev = _GENESIS
274        for row_id, proof_json, prev_hash, entry_hash in rows:
275            if prev_hash != prev:
276                return ChainVerification(
277                    False, len(rows), row_id,
278                    "prev_hash does not match the preceding record "
279                    "(deletion, insertion, or reordering)")
280            expected = _entry_hash(prev, proof_json)
281            if entry_hash != expected:
282                return ChainVerification(
283                    False, len(rows), row_id,
284                    "record contents do not match entry_hash (tampered)")
285            prev = entry_hash
286        if expected_head is not None and prev != expected_head:
287            return ChainVerification(
288                False, len(rows), None,
289                f"chain head {prev!r} does not match the trusted checkpoint "
290                f"{expected_head!r} (suffix rewrite or truncation)")
291        return ChainVerification(True, len(rows))
292
293    # ── Lifecycle ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
294
295    def close(self) -> None:
296        """Close the database connection."""
297        with self._lock:
298            if self._conn is not None:
299                self._conn.close()
300                self._conn = None
301
302    def __enter__(self) -> TarlAuditArchive:
303        return self
304
305    def __exit__(self, *_) -> None:
306        self.close()

Append-only audit log of TarlProof certificates backed by SQLite.

Thread-safe: concurrent stores and queries are serialised by an internal lock. Use the context-manager form to ensure the connection is closed properly::

with TarlAuditArchive("tarl_audit.db") as archive:
    archive.store(proof)

Or manage the lifecycle manually with close().

TarlAuditArchive(db_path: str = 'tarl_audit.db') 
66    def __init__(self, db_path: str = "tarl_audit.db") -> None:
67        self._path = db_path
68        self._conn: sqlite3.Connection | None = None
69        self._lock = threading.Lock()
70        self._ensure_schema()
def store( self, proof: TarlProof, expires_at: str | None = None) -> int: 
113    def store(
114        self,
115        proof: TarlProof,
116        expires_at: str | None = None,
117    ) -> int:
118        """
119        Persist a proof in the archive.
120
121        :param proof:       The TarlProof to store.
122        :param expires_at:  Optional ISO-8601 UTC expiry from TarlDecision.expires_at.
123        :returns:           Row id of the inserted record.
124        """
125        proof_json = proof.to_json()
126        with self._lock:
127            conn = self._connect()
128            # Link this record to the current chain head (genesis if empty).
129            head = conn.execute(
130                "SELECT entry_hash FROM proofs ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1"
131            ).fetchone()
132            prev_hash = head[0] if head and head[0] else _GENESIS
133            entry_hash = _entry_hash(prev_hash, proof_json)
134            cursor = conn.execute(
135                """INSERT INTO proofs
136                   (policy_hash, context_hash, verdict, rule_index,
137                    evaluated_at, expires_at, signature, proof_json,
138                    prev_hash, entry_hash)
139                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
140                (
141                    proof.policy_hash,
142                    proof.context_hash,
143                    proof.verdict.value,
144                    proof.rule_index,
145                    proof.evaluated_at,
146                    expires_at,
147                    proof.signature or None,
148                    proof_json,
149                    prev_hash,
150                    entry_hash,
151                ),
152            )
153            conn.commit()
154            # lastrowid is the new AUTOINCREMENT id after a successful INSERT;
155            # it is only None if no row was inserted (never on this path).
156            return cursor.lastrowid or 0

Persist a proof in the archive.

Parameters
  • proof: The TarlProof to store.
  • expires_at: Optional ISO-8601 UTC expiry from TarlDecision.expires_at. :returns: Row id of the inserted record.
def query( self, verdict: str | None = None, from_dt: str | None = None, to_dt: str | None = None, limit: int = 100, verifier=None) -> list[TarlProof]: 
160    def query(
161        self,
162        verdict: str | None = None,
163        from_dt: str | None = None,
164        to_dt: str | None = None,
165        limit: int = 100,
166        verifier=None,
167    ) -> list[TarlProof]:
168        """
169        Query stored proofs, newest first.
170
171        :param verdict:   Filter by verdict ("ALLOW", "DENY", or "ESCALATE").
172        :param from_dt:   ISO-8601 lower bound on evaluated_at (inclusive).
173        :param to_dt:     ISO-8601 upper bound on evaluated_at (inclusive).
174        :param limit:     Maximum number of results (default 100).
175        :param verifier:  Optional ProofVerifier.  When supplied, proofs whose
176                          signature check returns False (cryptographically
177                          invalid) are excluded from results.  Unsigned proofs
178                          (signature="") are kept; only actively invalid
179                          signatures are rejected.  Without a verifier, rows
180                          are returned as-is from SQLite with no tamper check
181                          — callers that need tamper-evidence must pass one.
182        :returns:         List of TarlProof objects.
183        """
184        conditions: list[str] = []
185        params: list = []
186        if verdict:
187            conditions.append("verdict = ?")
188            params.append(verdict.upper())
189        if from_dt:
190            conditions.append("evaluated_at >= ?")
191            params.append(from_dt)
192        if to_dt:
193            conditions.append("evaluated_at <= ?")
194            params.append(to_dt)
195        where = ("WHERE " + " AND ".join(conditions)) if conditions else ""
196        params.append(limit)
197        with self._lock:
198            conn = self._connect()
199            rows = conn.execute(
200                f"SELECT proof_json FROM proofs {where} "
201                f"ORDER BY evaluated_at DESC LIMIT ?",
202                params,
203            ).fetchall()
204        proofs = [TarlProof.from_json(row[0]) for row in rows]
205        if verifier is not None:
206            proofs = [
207                p for p in proofs
208                if verifier.verify(p).checks.get("signature") is not False
209            ]
210        return proofs

Query stored proofs, newest first.

Parameters
  • verdict: Filter by verdict ("ALLOW", "DENY", or "ESCALATE").
  • from_dt: ISO-8601 lower bound on evaluated_at (inclusive).
  • to_dt: ISO-8601 upper bound on evaluated_at (inclusive).
  • limit: Maximum number of results (default 100).
  • verifier: Optional ProofVerifier. When supplied, proofs whose signature check returns False (cryptographically invalid) are excluded from results. Unsigned proofs (signature="") are kept; only actively invalid signatures are rejected. Without a verifier, rows are returned as-is from SQLite with no tamper check — callers that need tamper-evidence must pass one. :returns: List of TarlProof objects.
def count( self, verdict: str | None = None, from_dt: str | None = None, to_dt: str | None = None) -> int: 
212    def count(
213        self,
214        verdict: str | None = None,
215        from_dt: str | None = None,
216        to_dt: str | None = None,
217    ) -> int:
218        """Count proofs matching the given filters."""
219        conditions: list[str] = []
220        params: list = []
221        if verdict:
222            conditions.append("verdict = ?")
223            params.append(verdict.upper())
224        if from_dt:
225            conditions.append("evaluated_at >= ?")
226            params.append(from_dt)
227        if to_dt:
228            conditions.append("evaluated_at <= ?")
229            params.append(to_dt)
230        where = ("WHERE " + " AND ".join(conditions)) if conditions else ""
231        with self._lock:
232            conn = self._connect()
233            row = conn.execute(
234                f"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM proofs {where}", params
235            ).fetchone()
236        return int(row[0]) if row else 0

Count proofs matching the given filters.

def head_hash(self) -> str: 
240    def head_hash(self) -> str:
241        """Return the current chain head (latest entry_hash), or genesis if empty.
242
243        Persist this externally as a checkpoint: a later ``verify_chain`` whose
244        head differs from a trusted checkpoint reveals suffix rewriting even if
245        the chain re-links internally."""
246        with self._lock:
247            conn = self._connect()
248            row = conn.execute(
249                "SELECT entry_hash FROM proofs ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1"
250            ).fetchone()
251        return str(row[0]) if row and row[0] else _GENESIS

Return the current chain head (latest entry_hash), or genesis if empty.

Persist this externally as a checkpoint: a later verify_chain whose head differs from a trusted checkpoint reveals suffix rewriting even if the chain re-links internally.

def verify_chain( self, expected_head: str | None = None) -> utf.tarl.archive.ChainVerification: 
253    def verify_chain(
254        self, expected_head: str | None = None
255    ) -> ChainVerification:
256        """Walk the hash chain in insertion order and report its integrity.
257
258        Detects record tampering (a modified ``proof_json`` no longer hashes to
259        its stored ``entry_hash``), deletion/reordering (a record's ``prev_hash``
260        no longer matches its predecessor's ``entry_hash``), and a tampered head.
261
262        ``expected_head`` is a trusted external checkpoint (see :meth:`head_hash`
263        and ``tarl audit checkpoint``). A chain that re-links internally but
264        whose head differs from the checkpoint reveals suffix rewriting or
265        truncation — the case the internal walk alone cannot catch.
266        """
267        with self._lock:
268            conn = self._connect()
269            rows = conn.execute(
270                "SELECT id, proof_json, prev_hash, entry_hash "
271                "FROM proofs ORDER BY id ASC"
272            ).fetchall()
273        prev = _GENESIS
274        for row_id, proof_json, prev_hash, entry_hash in rows:
275            if prev_hash != prev:
276                return ChainVerification(
277                    False, len(rows), row_id,
278                    "prev_hash does not match the preceding record "
279                    "(deletion, insertion, or reordering)")
280            expected = _entry_hash(prev, proof_json)
281            if entry_hash != expected:
282                return ChainVerification(
283                    False, len(rows), row_id,
284                    "record contents do not match entry_hash (tampered)")
285            prev = entry_hash
286        if expected_head is not None and prev != expected_head:
287            return ChainVerification(
288                False, len(rows), None,
289                f"chain head {prev!r} does not match the trusted checkpoint "
290                f"{expected_head!r} (suffix rewrite or truncation)")
291        return ChainVerification(True, len(rows))

Walk the hash chain in insertion order and report its integrity.

Detects record tampering (a modified proof_json no longer hashes to its stored entry_hash), deletion/reordering (a record's prev_hash no longer matches its predecessor's entry_hash), and a tampered head.

expected_head is a trusted external checkpoint (see head_hash() and tarl audit checkpoint). A chain that re-links internally but whose head differs from the checkpoint reveals suffix rewriting or truncation — the case the internal walk alone cannot catch.

def close(self) -> None: 
295    def close(self) -> None:
296        """Close the database connection."""
297        with self._lock:
298            if self._conn is not None:
299                self._conn.close()
300                self._conn = None

Close the database connection.

class TarlExplainer: 
 96class TarlExplainer:
 97    """
 98    Explains T.A.R.L. policy evaluations in human-readable form.
 99
100    Evaluates a policy rule-by-rule, recording why each rule matched or
101    did not, and returns a :class:`PolicyExplanation` with the full trace.
102
103    Usage::
104
105        explainer = TarlExplainer()
106        exp = explainer.explain({"user": {"role": "admin"}}, policy_text=src)
107        print(exp.format())
108    """
109
110    def explain(
111        self,
112        context: dict,
113        policy_text: str = "",
114        policy: TarlPolicy | None = None,
115    ) -> PolicyExplanation:
116        """
117        Evaluate a policy against context and return a full explanation.
118
119        :param context:     The evaluation context dict.
120        :param policy_text: Policy source text (used if policy is None).
121        :param policy:      Pre-parsed TarlPolicy (takes precedence).
122        """
123        from utf.tarl.core import PolicyParser, SafeExpr, _check_policy_temporal
124
125        if policy is None:
126            if not policy_text:
127                return PolicyExplanation(
128                    policy_name="unnamed",
129                    verdict=TarlVerdict.DENY,
130                    matched_rule_index=-1,
131                )
132            policy = PolicyParser.parse(policy_text)
133
134        # Temporal window check fires before any rule evaluation
135        temporal = _check_policy_temporal(policy)
136        if temporal is not None:
137            return PolicyExplanation(
138                policy_name=policy.name,
139                verdict=temporal.verdict,
140                matched_rule_index=-1,
141                temporal_reason=temporal.reason,
142            )
143
144        traces: list[RuleTrace] = []
145        matched_index = -1
146        expires_at = None
147
148        for i, rule in enumerate(policy.rules):
149            if matched_index >= 0:
150                traces.append(RuleTrace(
151                    rule_index=i,
152                    condition=rule.condition,
153                    verdict=rule.verdict,
154                    matched=False,
155                    evaluated=False,
156                ))
157                continue
158
159            error = None
160            matched = False
161            try:
162                matched = bool(SafeExpr.evaluate(rule.condition, context))
163            except Exception as exc:
164                error = str(exc)
165
166            traces.append(RuleTrace(
167                rule_index=i,
168                condition=rule.condition,
169                verdict=rule.verdict,
170                matched=matched,
171                evaluated=True,
172                error=error,
173            ))
174
175            if matched:
176                matched_index = i
177                if rule.duration_seconds:
178                    expires_at = (
179                        datetime.datetime.now(datetime.UTC)
180                        + datetime.timedelta(seconds=rule.duration_seconds)
181                    ).isoformat(timespec="seconds")
182
183        verdict = (
184            policy.rules[matched_index].verdict
185            if matched_index >= 0
186            else TarlVerdict.DENY
187        )
188
189        return PolicyExplanation(
190            policy_name=policy.name,
191            verdict=verdict,
192            matched_rule_index=matched_index,
193            rule_traces=traces,
194            expires_at=expires_at,
195        )

Explains T.A.R.L. policy evaluations in human-readable form.

Evaluates a policy rule-by-rule, recording why each rule matched or did not, and returns a PolicyExplanation with the full trace.

Usage::

explainer = TarlExplainer()
exp = explainer.explain({"user": {"role": "admin"}}, policy_text=src)
print(exp.format())
def explain( self, context: dict, policy_text: str = '', policy: TarlPolicy | None = None) -> PolicyExplanation: 
110    def explain(
111        self,
112        context: dict,
113        policy_text: str = "",
114        policy: TarlPolicy | None = None,
115    ) -> PolicyExplanation:
116        """
117        Evaluate a policy against context and return a full explanation.
118
119        :param context:     The evaluation context dict.
120        :param policy_text: Policy source text (used if policy is None).
121        :param policy:      Pre-parsed TarlPolicy (takes precedence).
122        """
123        from utf.tarl.core import PolicyParser, SafeExpr, _check_policy_temporal
124
125        if policy is None:
126            if not policy_text:
127                return PolicyExplanation(
128                    policy_name="unnamed",
129                    verdict=TarlVerdict.DENY,
130                    matched_rule_index=-1,
131                )
132            policy = PolicyParser.parse(policy_text)
133
134        # Temporal window check fires before any rule evaluation
135        temporal = _check_policy_temporal(policy)
136        if temporal is not None:
137            return PolicyExplanation(
138                policy_name=policy.name,
139                verdict=temporal.verdict,
140                matched_rule_index=-1,
141                temporal_reason=temporal.reason,
142            )
143
144        traces: list[RuleTrace] = []
145        matched_index = -1
146        expires_at = None
147
148        for i, rule in enumerate(policy.rules):
149            if matched_index >= 0:
150                traces.append(RuleTrace(
151                    rule_index=i,
152                    condition=rule.condition,
153                    verdict=rule.verdict,
154                    matched=False,
155                    evaluated=False,
156                ))
157                continue
158
159            error = None
160            matched = False
161            try:
162                matched = bool(SafeExpr.evaluate(rule.condition, context))
163            except Exception as exc:
164                error = str(exc)
165
166            traces.append(RuleTrace(
167                rule_index=i,
168                condition=rule.condition,
169                verdict=rule.verdict,
170                matched=matched,
171                evaluated=True,
172                error=error,
173            ))
174
175            if matched:
176                matched_index = i
177                if rule.duration_seconds:
178                    expires_at = (
179                        datetime.datetime.now(datetime.UTC)
180                        + datetime.timedelta(seconds=rule.duration_seconds)
181                    ).isoformat(timespec="seconds")
182
183        verdict = (
184            policy.rules[matched_index].verdict
185            if matched_index >= 0
186            else TarlVerdict.DENY
187        )
188
189        return PolicyExplanation(
190            policy_name=policy.name,
191            verdict=verdict,
192            matched_rule_index=matched_index,
193            rule_traces=traces,
194            expires_at=expires_at,
195        )

Evaluate a policy against context and return a full explanation.

Parameters
  • context: The evaluation context dict.
  • policy_text: Policy source text (used if policy is None).
  • policy: Pre-parsed TarlPolicy (takes precedence).
@dataclass
class PolicyExplanation: 
38@dataclass
39class PolicyExplanation:
40    """Full explanation of a policy evaluation."""
41    policy_name: str
42    verdict: TarlVerdict
43    matched_rule_index: int               # -1 = DEFAULT_DENY
44    rule_traces: list[RuleTrace] = field(default_factory=list)
45    temporal_reason: str | None = None
46    expires_at: str | None = None
47
48    def format(self, verbose: bool = False) -> str:
49        """
50        Return a human-readable explanation string.
51
52        :param verbose: When True, include skipped rules in the trace.
53        """
54        lines = [
55            f"Policy:   {self.policy_name}",
56            f"Verdict:  {self.verdict.value}",
57        ]
58        if self.matched_rule_index >= 0:
59            lines.append(f"Matched:  Rule #{self.matched_rule_index}")
60        else:
61            lines.append("Matched:  DEFAULT_DENY (no rule matched)")
62        if self.expires_at:
63            lines.append(f"Expires:  {self.expires_at}")
64        if self.temporal_reason:
65            lines.append(f"\nTemporal: {self.temporal_reason}")
66            return "\n".join(lines)
67        if not self.rule_traces:
68            return "\n".join(lines)
69        lines.append("\nRule evaluation trace:")
70        for trace in self.rule_traces:
71            if verbose or trace.evaluated:
72                lines.append(str(trace))
73        return "\n".join(lines)
74
75    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
76        return {
77            "policy_name": self.policy_name,
78            "verdict": self.verdict.value,
79            "matched_rule_index": self.matched_rule_index,
80            "expires_at": self.expires_at,
81            "temporal_reason": self.temporal_reason,
82            "rule_traces": [
83                {
84                    "rule_index": t.rule_index,
85                    "condition": t.condition,
86                    "verdict": t.verdict.value,
87                    "matched": t.matched,
88                    "evaluated": t.evaluated,
89                    "error": t.error,
90                }
91                for t in self.rule_traces
92            ],
93        }

Full explanation of a policy evaluation.

PolicyExplanation( policy_name: str, verdict: TarlVerdict, matched_rule_index: int, rule_traces: list[RuleTrace] = <factory>, temporal_reason: str | None = None, expires_at: str | None = None)
policy_name: str
verdict: TarlVerdict
matched_rule_index: int
rule_traces: list[RuleTrace]
temporal_reason: str | None = None
expires_at: str | None = None
def format(self, verbose: bool = False) -> str: 
48    def format(self, verbose: bool = False) -> str:
49        """
50        Return a human-readable explanation string.
51
52        :param verbose: When True, include skipped rules in the trace.
53        """
54        lines = [
55            f"Policy:   {self.policy_name}",
56            f"Verdict:  {self.verdict.value}",
57        ]
58        if self.matched_rule_index >= 0:
59            lines.append(f"Matched:  Rule #{self.matched_rule_index}")
60        else:
61            lines.append("Matched:  DEFAULT_DENY (no rule matched)")
62        if self.expires_at:
63            lines.append(f"Expires:  {self.expires_at}")
64        if self.temporal_reason:
65            lines.append(f"\nTemporal: {self.temporal_reason}")
66            return "\n".join(lines)
67        if not self.rule_traces:
68            return "\n".join(lines)
69        lines.append("\nRule evaluation trace:")
70        for trace in self.rule_traces:
71            if verbose or trace.evaluated:
72                lines.append(str(trace))
73        return "\n".join(lines)

Return a human-readable explanation string.

Parameters
  • verbose: When True, include skipped rules in the trace.
def to_dict(self) -> dict: 
75    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
76        return {
77            "policy_name": self.policy_name,
78            "verdict": self.verdict.value,
79            "matched_rule_index": self.matched_rule_index,
80            "expires_at": self.expires_at,
81            "temporal_reason": self.temporal_reason,
82            "rule_traces": [
83                {
84                    "rule_index": t.rule_index,
85                    "condition": t.condition,
86                    "verdict": t.verdict.value,
87                    "matched": t.matched,
88                    "evaluated": t.evaluated,
89                    "error": t.error,
90                }
91                for t in self.rule_traces
92            ],
93        }
@dataclass
class RuleTrace: 
16@dataclass
17class RuleTrace:
18    """Evaluation trace for a single rule."""
19    rule_index: int
20    condition: str
21    verdict: TarlVerdict
22    matched: bool
23    evaluated: bool = True       # False if skipped — earlier rule already matched
24    error: str | None = None
25
26    def __str__(self) -> str:
27        if not self.evaluated:
28            return f"  [{self.rule_index}] (skipped — earlier rule matched)"
29        status = "MATCHED" if self.matched else "no match"
30        err = f"  [error: {self.error}]" if self.error else ""
31        return (
32            f"  [{self.rule_index}] when {self.condition}"
33            f" => {self.verdict.value}"
34            f"  →  {status}{err}"
35        )

Evaluation trace for a single rule.

RuleTrace( rule_index: int, condition: str, verdict: TarlVerdict, matched: bool, evaluated: bool = True, error: str | None = None)
rule_index: int
condition: str
verdict: TarlVerdict
matched: bool
evaluated: bool = True
error: str | None = None
class TarlTestRunner: 
113class TarlTestRunner:
114    """
115    Parses and executes .tarl_test suites.
116
117    Usage::
118
119        runner = TarlTestRunner()
120
121        # Single file
122        result = runner.run_file("tests/access.tarl_test")
123        print(result)
124
125        # All .tarl_test files under a directory (recursive)
126        for result in runner.run_directory("tests/policies/"):
127            print(result)
128    """
129
130    def run_file(self, path: str) -> TarlTestSuiteResult:
131        """Run a single .tarl_test file."""
132        base_dir = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path))
133        try:
134            with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
135                text = fh.read()
136        except OSError as exc:
137            return TarlTestSuiteResult(
138                file_path=path, total=0, passed=0, failed=0,
139                results=[], load_error=str(exc),
140            )
141        return self._run_text(text, base_dir=base_dir, file_path=path)
142
143    def run_directory(self, path: str) -> list[TarlTestSuiteResult]:
144        """
145        Recursively find and run all .tarl_test files under path.
146        Returns results in sorted order by file path.
147        """
148        pattern = os.path.join(path, "**", "*.tarl_test")
149        paths = sorted(_glob.glob(pattern, recursive=True))
150        return [self.run_file(p) for p in paths]
151
152    def run_text(
153        self,
154        text: str,
155        base_dir: str = ".",
156        file_path: str = "<inline>",
157    ) -> TarlTestSuiteResult:
158        """Parse and run test cases from a text string."""
159        return self._run_text(text, base_dir=base_dir, file_path=file_path)
160
161    # ── Internal ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
162
163    def _run_text(
164        self,
165        text: str,
166        base_dir: str,
167        file_path: str,
168    ) -> TarlTestSuiteResult:
169        try:
170            suite = self._parse(text)
171        except ValueError as exc:
172            return TarlTestSuiteResult(
173                file_path=file_path, total=0, passed=0, failed=0,
174                results=[], load_error=str(exc),
175            )
176
177        policy_text: str | None = suite.policy_text
178        if policy_text is None and suite.policy_file:
179            ppath = (
180                suite.policy_file if os.path.isabs(suite.policy_file)
181                else os.path.join(base_dir, suite.policy_file)
182            )
183            try:
184                with open(ppath, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
185                    policy_text = fh.read()
186            except OSError as exc:
187                return TarlTestSuiteResult(
188                    file_path=file_path, total=0, passed=0, failed=0,
189                    results=[],
190                    load_error=f"Cannot open policy_file {ppath!r}: {exc}",
191                )
192
193        if policy_text is None:
194            return TarlTestSuiteResult(
195                file_path=file_path, total=0, passed=0, failed=0,
196                results=[],
197                load_error="No policy_file or inline policy defined",
198            )
199
200        policy = PolicyParser.parse(policy_text)
201        results: list[TarlTestResult] = []
202        for tc in suite.tests:
203            results.append(self._run_case(tc, policy))
204
205        passed = sum(1 for r in results if r.passed)
206        return TarlTestSuiteResult(
207            file_path=file_path,
208            total=len(results),
209            passed=passed,
210            failed=len(results) - passed,
211            results=results,
212        )
213
214    def _run_case(self, tc: TarlTestCase, policy) -> TarlTestResult:
215        try:
216            decision = evaluate_policy(tc.context, policy=policy)
217        except Exception as exc:
218            return TarlTestResult(
219                name=tc.name,
220                passed=False,
221                expected=tc.expect,
222                actual=TarlVerdict.DENY,
223                expected_rule=tc.expect_rule,
224                actual_rule=-1,
225                error=f"Evaluation error: {exc}",
226            )
227
228        verdict_ok = decision.verdict == tc.expect
229        rule_ok = (
230            tc.expect_rule is None
231            or decision.rule_index == tc.expect_rule
232        )
233        return TarlTestResult(
234            name=tc.name,
235            passed=verdict_ok and rule_ok,
236            expected=tc.expect,
237            actual=decision.verdict,
238            expected_rule=tc.expect_rule,
239            actual_rule=decision.rule_index,
240        )
241
242    def _parse(self, text: str) -> TarlTestFile:
243        """
244        Parse .tarl_test text into a TarlTestFile.
245
246        Raises ValueError on malformed directives.
247        """
248        suite = TarlTestFile()
249        lines = text.split("\n")
250        n = len(lines)
251        i = 0
252        current_test: dict | None = None
253        inline_mode = False   # True when collecting indented policy lines
254
255        def _flush() -> None:
256            if current_test is None:
257                return
258            name = current_test.get("name", "unnamed")
259            raw_ctx = current_test.get("context", "{}")
260            try:
261                ctx = json.loads(raw_ctx)
262            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
263                raise ValueError(
264                    f"Invalid JSON context in test {name!r}: {exc}"
265                ) from exc
266            raw_expect = current_test.get("expect", "")
267            try:
268                expect = TarlVerdict(raw_expect.upper())
269            except ValueError as exc:
270                raise ValueError(
271                    f"Invalid expect verdict {raw_expect!r} in test {name!r}."
272                    f" Must be ALLOW, DENY, or ESCALATE."
273                ) from exc
