274            raw_rule = current_test.get("expect_rule")
275            expect_rule = int(raw_rule) if raw_rule is not None else None
276            suite.tests.append(TarlTestCase(
277                name=name,
278                context=ctx,
279                expect=expect,
280                expect_rule=expect_rule,
281                source_line=current_test.get("line", 0),
282            ))
283
284        inline_policy_lines: list[str] = []
285
286        while i < n:
287            raw = lines[i]
288            stripped = raw.strip()
289            i += 1
290
291            if not stripped or stripped.startswith("#"):
292                if inline_mode:
293                    inline_policy_lines.append("")
294                continue
295
296            # Collecting inline policy block
297            if inline_mode:
298                if raw.startswith(("  ", "\t")):
299                    inline_policy_lines.append(stripped)
300                    continue
301                else:
302                    # Non-indented line ends the inline policy block
303                    inline_mode = False
304                    while inline_policy_lines and not inline_policy_lines[-1]:
305                        inline_policy_lines.pop()
306                    suite.policy_text = "\n".join(inline_policy_lines)
307
308            if stripped.startswith("policy_file:"):
309                suite.policy_file = stripped[len("policy_file:"):].strip()
310                continue
311
312            if stripped == "policy:" or stripped.startswith("policy: |"):
313                inline_mode = True
314                inline_policy_lines = []
315                continue
316
317            if stripped.startswith("test ") and stripped.endswith(":"):
318                _flush()
319                current_test = {"line": i}
320                rest = stripped[5:-1].strip()
321                if (rest.startswith('"') and rest.endswith('"')) or \
322                   (rest.startswith("'") and rest.endswith("'")):
323                    rest = rest[1:-1]
324                current_test["name"] = rest
325                continue
326
327            if current_test is not None:
328                if stripped.startswith("context:"):
329                    current_test["context"] = stripped[len("context:"):].strip()
330                elif stripped.startswith("expect_rule:"):
331                    current_test["expect_rule"] = stripped[len("expect_rule:"):].strip()
332                elif stripped.startswith("expect:"):
333                    current_test["expect"] = stripped[len("expect:"):].strip()
334
335        # End of file: close any open inline policy block
336        if inline_mode:
337            while inline_policy_lines and not inline_policy_lines[-1]:
338                inline_policy_lines.pop()
339            suite.policy_text = "\n".join(inline_policy_lines)
340
341        _flush()
342        return suite

Parses and executes .tarl_test suites.

Usage::

runner = TarlTestRunner()

# Single file
result = runner.run_file("tests/access.tarl_test")
print(result)

# All .tarl_test files under a directory (recursive)
for result in runner.run_directory("tests/policies/"):
    print(result)
def run_file(self, path: str) -> TarlTestSuiteResult: 
130    def run_file(self, path: str) -> TarlTestSuiteResult:
131        """Run a single .tarl_test file."""
132        base_dir = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path))
133        try:
134            with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
135                text = fh.read()
136        except OSError as exc:
137            return TarlTestSuiteResult(
138                file_path=path, total=0, passed=0, failed=0,
139                results=[], load_error=str(exc),
140            )
141        return self._run_text(text, base_dir=base_dir, file_path=path)

Run a single .tarl_test file.

def run_directory(self, path: str) -> list[TarlTestSuiteResult]: 
143    def run_directory(self, path: str) -> list[TarlTestSuiteResult]:
144        """
145        Recursively find and run all .tarl_test files under path.
146        Returns results in sorted order by file path.
147        """
148        pattern = os.path.join(path, "**", "*.tarl_test")
149        paths = sorted(_glob.glob(pattern, recursive=True))
150        return [self.run_file(p) for p in paths]

Recursively find and run all .tarl_test files under path. Returns results in sorted order by file path.

def run_text( self, text: str, base_dir: str = '.', file_path: str = '<inline>') -> TarlTestSuiteResult: 
152    def run_text(
153        self,
154        text: str,
155        base_dir: str = ".",
156        file_path: str = "<inline>",
157    ) -> TarlTestSuiteResult:
158        """Parse and run test cases from a text string."""
159        return self._run_text(text, base_dir=base_dir, file_path=file_path)

Parse and run test cases from a text string.

@dataclass
class TarlTestSuiteResult: 
 89@dataclass
 90class TarlTestSuiteResult:
 91    """Aggregated results for a single .tarl_test file."""
 92    file_path: str
 93    total: int
 94    passed: int
 95    failed: int
 96    results: list[TarlTestResult]
 97    load_error: str | None = None
 98
 99    def __str__(self) -> str:
100        if self.load_error:
101            return f"ERROR  {self.file_path}: {self.load_error}"
102        header = f"{self.file_path}: {self.passed}/{self.total} passed"
103        lines = [header]
104        for r in self.results:
105            lines.append(str(r))
106        return "\n".join(lines)
107
108    @property
109    def ok(self) -> bool:
110        return self.load_error is None and self.failed == 0

Aggregated results for a single .tarl_test file.

TarlTestSuiteResult( file_path: str, total: int, passed: int, failed: int, results: list[TarlTestResult], load_error: str | None = None)
file_path: str
total: int
passed: int
failed: int
results: list[TarlTestResult]
load_error: str | None = None
ok: bool 
108    @property
109    def ok(self) -> bool:
110        return self.load_error is None and self.failed == 0
@dataclass
class TarlTestResult: 
63@dataclass
64class TarlTestResult:
65    """Result of running a single test case."""
66    name: str
67    passed: bool
68    expected: TarlVerdict
69    actual: TarlVerdict
70    expected_rule: int | None = None
71    actual_rule: int = -1
72    error: str | None = None
73
74    def __str__(self) -> str:
75        status = "PASS" if self.passed else "FAIL"
76        if self.passed:
77            return f"  {status}  {self.name}"
78        if self.error:
79            return f"  {status}  {self.name}  [{self.error}]"
80        detail = f"expected {self.expected.value}, got {self.actual.value}"
81        if self.expected_rule is not None:
82            detail += (
83                f"; rule expected {self.expected_rule},"
84                f" got {self.actual_rule}"
85            )
86        return f"  {status}  {self.name}  [{detail}]"

Result of running a single test case.

TarlTestResult( name: str, passed: bool, expected: TarlVerdict, actual: TarlVerdict, expected_rule: int | None = None, actual_rule: int = -1, error: str | None = None)
name: str
passed: bool
expected: TarlVerdict
actual: TarlVerdict
expected_rule: int | None = None
actual_rule: int = -1
error: str | None = None
@dataclass
class TarlTestCase: 
45@dataclass
46class TarlTestCase:
47    """A single test case parsed from a .tarl_test file."""
48    name: str
49    context: dict
50    expect: TarlVerdict
51    expect_rule: int | None = None
52    source_line: int = 0

A single test case parsed from a .tarl_test file.

TarlTestCase( name: str, context: dict, expect: TarlVerdict, expect_rule: int | None = None, source_line: int = 0)
name: str
context: dict
expect: TarlVerdict
expect_rule: int | None = None
source_line: int = 0
